sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content:
Hi, good morning, thank you very much. Welcome to our results conference call. We are very glad to share our 2013 results with you. This was our first year as an investment company. We were very successful on our IPO and also successful on stock returns so far, especially considering the general Brazilian market's performance. This year resulted in an impressive BRL 207 million of net income. Our fourth quarter was especially rewarding with 35% year-over-year growth on miles accrued by our partners, except GOL. Our margins with Smiles & Money and miles projects endured with strength and consistence. 
Now on the quarter. The net profit reached almost BRL 67 million, growing almost 6% quarter-over-quarter. We intend to pay a 95% payout over our 2013 results. It was the result of an approximation with the financial marketing -- markets, developed joint projects with every bank in Brazil, and also, developing a very innovative project highlighting our Clube Smiles, our Smiles Club, the purchase, the reactivation and the transferring of miles, among other products. We kept, especially with Smiles & Money, the pioneering spirit of -- and consistent growth in this product share in the company. It all resulted in high engagement of our customers and significant increase in miles accrued. Additionally, we partnered with important companies, and it is worth to highlight our partnership with PayPal, worldwide leader in online payments. PayPal made Smiles the first program to offer international retail redemption.  Also in the retail segment, we are very proud with our investment did in Netpoints. 
In our opinion, the best way to have, in the future, a strong relevance with retailers in the Brazilian market, allowing us to keep our focus in the frequent flyers in Smiles. We also went back to the media with significant ad campaign emphasizing that Smiles is the preferred program among frequent flyers in Brazil. One of the main importance tasks for last year was how close we stayed with GOL, strengthen our relationship in our corporate governance. GOL remains our main airline partner responsible for roughly 9% of our redemptions. Our strategic alignment allowed us to develop joint programs and products for our customers. 
Not only are we thinking and bringing new international partners, but we are also strengthening our relationship with our current partners: Delta, Air France, Iberia, KLM and Qatar. All in all, 2013 was a consistent, rewarding and challenging year. We ended the year delivering a performance according to our shareholders' and customers' expectations. We are prepared and motivated for a very challenged 2014, especially considering the unoptimistic macroeconomic and other factors we are likely to face with a muted consumer environment and harder competitive futures.
So I'd like to introduce Flavio Vargas, our CFO, and Flavio is going to talk a little bit about our financial performance.

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content:
Hi. Thank you, Leonel, and good morning, everyone, and thank you for participating in our fourth quarter earnings conference call. We are very happy to share the comments for our 2013 results with you. 
Initially, we are very pleased with our first year performance as an independent company, and we believe that we have met the expectations for our IPO in terms of metrics for the program. We enjoyed a strong performance regardless -- regarding miles accrued and redemption growth, impacting revenues and gross billings positively during the year. We achieved a net profit of BRL 66.7 million in the fourth quarter and BRL 207.8 million for the full year of 2013. We posted net revenues of BRL 187.6 million in the fourth quarter, which was up 20% over the previous quarter. Our strong financial results were mainly due to our decision to advance ticket purchase and financial revenues from financial investments. It's worth mentioning that the recent U.S. dollar appreciation did not impact our unit price of miles as one would expect, due to the adversity of miles to financial partner in the amount of BRL 400 million in April last year. 
Well, moving to Slide #4, we present the evolution of our gross billings and miles accrued. We reached BRL 1 billion of gross billings in 2013, which was up 10.3% on a year-on-year basis. Speaking about the last quarter, our gross billings were BRL 286 million, up 7.3% over the previous quarterly, chiefly due to the significant growth in Smiles & Money gross billings. We closed the year with 38.8 billion miles accrued, up 8.2% over 2012. If we consider all partners excluding GOL in the fourth quarter, we grew an outstanding 35.2% over the previous year. 
Moving to Slide 5, we highlight the strong engagement of our customers and the attractiveness of our program. We grew 16.8% over 2012 on redemptions, with 30.7 billion miles redeemed in 2013 and awarding almost 4 million ticket and products over the year.
On Slide 6, we present the performance of the products margin. Our margin with traditional awards, which are awards redeemed on a 100% miles basis, reached 40.6%, up 13.2 percentage points over the previous quarter. The Smiles & Money margin increased from 38.9% to 46.9%, in line with the first half of the last year, with an increase in the product share within the company's portfolio. On Slide 7, we present our breakage rate. We ended this quarter with a 15.5% breakage rate, which we believe reflects a good balance of profitability and effectiveness of the program. 
Moving to Slide 8, we present our net income. We obtained net revenues of BRL 187.6 million on the fourth quarter. Broken down, being BRL 100 million for miles redemptions and BRL 79 million from Smiles & Money. Our cost of redemption and operational expenses totaled BRL 131 million on the quarter, leading to an operating margin of 30% and an operating income of BRL 36.3 million. Our net profit for the quarter was BRL 66.7 million, representing a margin of 35.6%. 
On Slide 9, we present the performance of our shares since the IPO. We obtained a 50% gain, while Bovespa Index was approximately 5% negative during the same period. In line with our commitment to shareholders, our board proposed a payment of additional BRL 160.3 million on dividends to be approved at our shareholders' meeting scheduled for April. Additionally, we have already paid BRL 37.1 million in dividends in the second half, which should total almost BRL 200 million, representing a payout of 95%.
So I'd now like to thank you all for the attention and open the floor for the Q&A session. Thank you.

sections.speaker: Mario Pierry
sections.content: Let me ask you 2 questions. The first one is related to miles, so to GOL. I think I saw an increase of about 38% year-on-year while we were seeing this balance actually declining the previous quarter. So if you could explain why such a sharp increase to GOL? And also, when do you expect to see the impact of the new accrual program implemented at GOL, when should we see an impact from that? The second question is related to the balance of the miles that you presold to the 3 banks at the beginning of 2013. If you could give us, what is the balance remaining and when do you expect this balance to expire?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Mario, this is Leonel. Thanks a lot for your questions. Let me answer your first question. Related to GOL, what we did was just an incentive program for the last months -- the last 2 months in the year. You know that GOL changed the program and we are just building with GOL a relationship, a commercial relationship, similar that we have with the banks. So we developed some programs, some incentives, some opportunity -- we see some opportunity to increase the redemption then increase the engagement of the customers. We just did it. Of course, that when we see GOL in the months ahead, probably GOL will remain in a conservative accrual in the future, because they changed the program. They became the program bearer for the frequent flyer, but at the same time now they reduced the offering for the non-frequent flyers, which, in our opinion, is correct, is good for the engagement in the future. Because for the frequent flyers, the program now is very, very -- is much better, is very competitive. They increased the competitiveness. So all in all, I think that we -- what we did is just an incentive program now, like we have with banks. And in the future, GOL, we can develop again or not in the future, something like that. But for their -- via GOL, we'll have probably something with the same amount -- a little bit less than they had in 2013. For your second question, Flavio will explain better.

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: Yes. Mario, thank you for participating and thank you for the questions. The balance that we have as of December 31 is around BRL 170 million is still outstanding. Considering that the current run rate and our expectation, probably most of this balance is going to be over by the end of the first half, and probably around -- between BRL 15 million and BRL 20 million much less until the end of the year.

sections.speaker: Mario Pierry
sections.content: Okay, very clear answers. Just to clarify then. This BRL 170 million still reflects average price of BRL 2, correct? And then we should eventually see the prices move up?

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: Yes, that's exactly right. It's still -- this BRL 170 million, they are part of the advance where we fixed the foreign exchange at around BRL 2 per dollar.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: My first question is very simple. Do you intend to provide any type of guidance given that this is the first year you are initiating as a public company?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: We had the discussions whether we should have a formal guidance policy, but we haven't made a final decision. So the answer right now is no. We currently don't have guidance. But we are analyzing the merits whether we should pursue that strategy or not.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: Okay. And if you'll allow me a second question, I just want to understand better the dynamics in your G&A expenses this quarter. It was really anticipated by you in the third Q that we should have a stronger fourth quarter because of some -- the execution of some marketing campaigns. I just want to understand what type of dynamics can we expect going forward? Also, can we see, in the fourth quarter of 2014, for instance, again, a concentration of marketing expenses, or will that be more spread through the whole year of 2014?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Alexandre, this is Leonel. I think what we did in the fourth quarter, it's just one-shot action because we needed to relaunch the Smiles brand in the markets. From now on, probably we have flat investments. We don't expect some big flow in the market investments. Probably this year, we are going to have investments along the year and we put our energy and our investments in terms of social media and Internet and relationship programs, activations, so not in branding. You know that's normally branding actions, in brands that we need to invest a lot for a short period. And we are not to going to do something like that in 2014.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: Okay. Does that mean that in 2014 you may spend less money with marketing than you did in 2013, or it's too early to assume that?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: No. We are going to have similar investments, but flat along the year.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: Okay. And one final question. You spent around BRL 20 million in 2013, does that make sense?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: It was what we had last year, and this year, we have something similar. A little bit more, but just a little bit more. But if you will consider that last year we just invested in the second half, this year, we are going to invest along the year and a little bit more than we had last year, but in a different way.

sections.speaker: Eduardo Couto
sections.content: I have 2 questions, if I may. The first one is in the margin side. You reported 50% gross margin, very strong margin. So how do you see margins going forward? And do you think this 40% to 50% level is something sustainable? That's the first question.

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: I think, Eduardo -- thank you, this is Flavio speaking. The discussions and the questions on margin, I think we have been averaging quite consistently over the previous quarter. I think it's natural that the margins fluctuate, sometimes you have some better quarters, sometimes we have some worse quarters. But our view is that the margins should fluctuate around what we have experienced over the average of the year, maybe on the -- more to the medium- to long-term view, a little bit lower than that. But our commitment is continue working on the margin side, continue working on having positive strength on the redemptions to keep the margin consistent and not fluctuate as much as we didn't take care of.

sections.speaker: Eduardo Couto
sections.content: Okay. No, makes sense. And just a second question, Flavio, regarding your cash position. You grew your cash position more than BRL 100 million during the fourth quarter, closed the year at BRL 400 million, or close to that. For this year, we expect the company to continue to grow its cash position. Excluding potential shareholders' payment, you would have more than BRL 1 billion by the end of this year. So I just want to know if the -- all this cash that you're generating, the main use for this cash is pretty much to return it to the shareholders or as dividends or as a capital reduction, that's the company's future?

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: I think that's pretty much the management commitment, to try to pursue what is the best for the investors. We have this quarter and this year, we are paying almost BRL 200 million in dividend, which is a 95% payout, which is pretty much the limit on what we can pay according to the Brazilian regulation. And this commitment remains. There's a possibility of the capital redemption, this is something that we are discussing with the board. But at the end of the day, this is a matter that needs to be approved at the shareholders' meeting. And we are working internally with the board, with the external providers of funding to make a formal recommendation to be approved at the shareholders' meeting.

sections.speaker: Eduardo Couto
sections.content: Okay. But you expect this strong cash generation to continue throughout the year, right?

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: Yes, yes.

sections.speaker: Gabriel Gusan
sections.content: My question is about the breakage revenues in the quarter. We saw there was a BRL 3.2 million entry for expiration of miles, which we're expecting to see, only much later. Can you please comment on that?

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: Well yes, Gabriel, and thank you for the questions. And breakage is definitely something that we always keep on the radar. And we are trying to make decisions on how we can try to manage and be more creative and rational in all our promotions. Basically, the main effect that we have on the quarter is connected to the joint promotion that we have won with GOL, where we were granting bonus miles, but we have decided to make the accrual and the redemption linked on the promotion, so the bonus miles had a very short -- the life of the miles had a very short lifespan. So that's why we had the gentle expiration of miles, which according to the regular program rules would expire only in 2 years. So we would try to be more creative to manage our cost and manage our promotions more efficiently.

sections.speaker: Gabriel Gusan
sections.content: Okay. Should we expect something for first quarter of 2014 as well?

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: We are -- right now, this is something that we have tested with GOL, this new model of having different life for the miles that we bonus the clients. And probably, this is something that we might start doing with the other partners in the future. We don't have anything on this right now. But the principle of trying to manage the breakage and manage the overall cost of promotions is one of the priorities that we have at the company.

sections.speaker: Victor Mizusaki
sections.content: Just as a quick question on the incorporation of G.A. Smiles. I mean, apparently this incorporation will generate a goodwill about BRL 214 million with NPV of BRL 73 million. So I don't know if you can comment a little bit about your effective tax rate in the coming years?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: So, Victor, thank you very much for the questions. The net effect of the incorporation of G.A. Smiles is that we created a goodwill, and we also created this -- the goodwill is going to be amortized over a 5-year period and that is going to generate over this period around BRL 72 million of tax shields and tax credits over this period, so that's what you can expect.

sections.speaker: Victor Mizusaki
sections.content: Okay, but in terms of effective tax rate, I don't know if you can comment what can I assume your model and -- I mean, the amortization would be linear or...

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Probably it's going to be linear. So because we generate sufficient profits to amortize this BRL 72 million over 60 months, so our expectations to be -- to have the same level of organic [indiscernible] over the course of these 5 years.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content:
Thanks a lot. I just like to add a few comments. The first one is that we have our commitment -- you have our commitment to remain as an innovative company this year. We are going to develop new products and new programs with our partners, especially in terms of redemption. At the same time, we are very proud to have the partnership with GOL. We have very good [ph] goals, and increasing our performance together and at the same time develop promotions, products and incentives to our customers.
And finally, it's important to mention that we concluded our restructuring. Now we have a team, complete team and a very good team in place. And we are ready to increase our competitiveness in the market. This year -- it seems that this year we have a very hard year in Brazil and -- but we remain confident that we have capacity to compete very well in the market. And you have our commitment that we are going to deliver another good year, probably growing less than -- of course, less than in 2013 because now we have a different level in the company, in the marketing, but confident that we can deliver a good year again.
So thank you. Thanks a lot and you can count on us anytime. Thank you.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content:
Hi, everyone. Good morning. Thanks for coming to our conference. It's a pleasure to present Smiles results for the first quarter of 2014. 
Following in Slide 3, we had net profit of BRL 78.8 million in the first quarter this year, with the margin about 40%. 16 percentage points higher compared to last year and 6 percentage points higher compared to last quarter. Our gross billing to region BRL 264 million, up 10% over last year and we had 9.2 billion miles accrued, flat with last year. It’s important to mention that our gross billings were not that affected by the exchange rate. Because there was a lot of miles sold in advance, in the second quarter last year. We have locked extended rate.
On the last general shareholders meeting, our  capital reduction of BRL 1 billion was approved which is very good news for our shareholders, which means BRL 8.19 per share, seeking the optional capital issued to republic shareholders. We also are proud to announce 2 new partnerships, Aerolíneas Argentinas and TAP, the Portuguese Airlines Company. Aerolíneas is already operational for the accrual and redemption of miles and the agreement with TAP was timing during the second quarter. TAP will be operational by the end of the second quarter this year.
One year ago, when Smiles offered its customer 560 destinations and with TAP, we will offer more than 1700 destination of options. We remain focused on our customers with innovation in mind. This quarter we launched our project [indiscernible] booking in English, unique product in Brazilian market. We also launched our mobile site, so our customers can issue tickets in a simple and fast way. We also had approval from the regulators for our minority acquisition of Netpoints, which I will detail ahead. 
Going forward, in Slide 4, I'd like to mention that we grew 10.5% over last year in terms of gross billing and we were 7% shorter over last quarter, especially due to the seasonality. It's important to mention that flat growth in miles accrued good news, considering that we have improved our margins to be special. Even if we had difference comparison basis last year, as one of our partners, Itaú announced the change of rules speaking with first quarter last year unfavorably. We are very confident, we will go back to growth in the second quarter and miles accrued. In fact we are growing a lot this month and in April we had a very good performance, in May we are growing a lot again.
In the Slide 5, we can see that the miles redemption growth of 6% over last year indication that our customers engagement to the program. 
In the Slide 6, we announced our partnership with Aerolíneas Argentinas and we feel we became the dominant program between Brazil and Argentina, the second largest destination for Brazilians. Also, with that the main company that operates between Brazil and Europe, we will become a strong loyal program along with KLM, Air France and Iberia. And we remain focused on airline partnership with 3 or more coming up in the next month. 
All in all, we'll reach more than 700 destinations with the addition of TAP, a new pack for the program. 
In the Slide 7, Aerolíneas is operational and we have some of this company’s relevant information. We have important destination within Argentina and in also in South America.
In Slide 8, we continue focus on innovation and customer experience. And the new online book progress allows our customers to reserve their seats for 3 days so they talk to their families before confirming or in the case they wanted to transfer purchase or reactivate their miles later. Our customers were cautious about transferring their miles and then issuing their tickets. But here they can book and then accrue their points. The first indicators of this product performing very, very well. 
In the Slide 9, our mobile website allows our customers to check their flight, issue GOL ticket, join the program and also join our Smiles club in a quick and easy platform everywhere, anytime.
In the Slide 10, now I'm going to talk about Netpoints. I have no doubts that Netpoints will soon become the best and biggest retail coalition largest company in Brazil. Together we have the best value proposition of the market. Together, we have 15.1 million potential customers with lead overlap, and we will use our base to releverage our partners’ gross billings. Netpoints grew in number of members, but more importantly, grew in terms of engagement 500% more exchanges compared to last year. I think that soon we will have very good news about Netpoints. And we had announced that one month ago -- some weeks ago we have announced that Netpoints have a new partner, our new shareholder, which is larger Lojas Marisa. Lojas Marisa is a very big retail chain around the country. So exactly after we have the license from the regulators, Lojas Marisa we will transfer their large balance into Netpoints. So now Flavio Vargas will speak a little more about this quarter's financial and I am available for questions after that. Thank you, very much.

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content:
Good morning, everyone, and thank you very much Leonel. For me it's also a pleasure to be here today to present the Smiles' first quarter results. We are very happy that on this quarter we had a net income of BRL 78.3 million, which is 17.5% higher than the last quarter and 162% higher over the last year. We ended this first quarter with net revenues worth of BRL 188 million, which also represents a strong growth of 61.3%, when compared to last year and pretty much flat when compared to the last quarter. 
When you talk about the gross billings, it's worth mentioning that the recent exchange rate did not have a substantial impact in the amount of the gross billings, as a substantial amount of the miles that we sell to the financial partners have been anticipated last year in connection with the IPO. Then jumping to Slide 8, we present the direct margins of our products. We're very happy that we have been able to show consistent results in line with our expectations showing that combined margin for the products which are 100% Smiles product and the Smiles & Money products which was 0.5 percentage points higher than compared to last year.
Going to Slide #12, we presented breakage for the first quarter this year. We ended the quarter with a breakage rate of 16.9%, which is 1.4 percentage points higher over last quarter. The increase in the breakage rate is chiefly explained due to the promotional accrual that we have had over this quarter. Using promotional miles with different expiration time has been one of the levels that we've been using to test and to minimize the cost per miles in connection with the promotional activity. 
Going to Slide #12, we break down the quarterly results. We obtained net revenues of BRL 188.1 million, with BRL 112 million worth of miles coming from the redemption of net revenue, and BRL 70 million coming from this Smiles & Money product. Our redemption cost and operational expenses were also very consistent and controlled and totaled BRL 119 million, which led to a BRL 59 million operating profit representing a 36.7% margin over the period. In a nutshell, we ended up reaching BRL 78.3 million of profit with a 41.6% margin. 
Going to Slide #14, we represent the performance of our stock since the IPO. We have been at a positive result and return of nearly 100%, while represented for the same period presented a negative result of 4.4%. In line with our commitment with the shareholders, the general shareholders meeting approved the confirmation of BRL 160 million dividend, which is going to be paid on July -- on May 14. And we also approved a BRL 1 billion capital reduction, which represents an extraordinary payment of BRL 8.19 per share.
The effective date for the capital reduction is going to be announced later on, after the 60-day legal period for the creditor opposition, hence which is going to be in the beginning of July. 
I hereby end the comments regarding the first quarter results for this year, and open the floor for the Q&A. Thank you very much.

sections.speaker: Catherine O'Brien
sections.content: This is Catherine O'Brien filling in for Mike. I just had couple of question on the impact of adding a partner airline. I was just wondering from the time that you implement a reciprocal accrual redemption agreement with the partner airline, how quickly do see an impact from your members using partner airline flights to accrue miles points. And I was wondering if you could just put some numbers on this financial impact of adding an airline partner?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Thank you for your question. This is Leonel. In average, we are talking with lots of possible partners. In average, between the contract and the operations, we spent some months, nominally between 4 and 6 months and this is our priority. In fact, in terms of redemption, the international partners have the best opportunity since we're growing more in the international than in local redemptions. Probably, we're going to grow something like more than 50% this year. But to be honest, I don't have at this moment the number that we are expecting. And lot to be representing in this Smiles program, but this is our priority. Let me ask if Flavio can add some comments.

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: When you talk about the implementation and how effective it is, in terms of timing. Definitely, the redemption is much more, you can see the results much faster, because once the redemption is implemented, it automatically appears in the online season for reservations and bookings. So whenever you add a new partnership, automatically you add more availability to the client and then the case starts with you. When you talk about the accrual, the accrual, it's a more the creation of, you need more of the creation from the customer, because they had to go -- it’s actually exactly goes to the airline, whenever this following one of the airline partners, and they have to request the miles should be accrued. So it usually it lags some time and  still people realize that the partnership is effective and they start to request the accrue of miles. But then, in terms of the financial impact, as of today the amount of redemption that we had coming from international partners, they still represent less than 5% of the total redemption. So that was something that expanding the amount of partnerships and expanding the amount of availability it has -- potentially it can have a good impact especially for the more affluent people and they can afford a more heavy users for the credit card industry compliance, because they have in terms of expectations, having the international redemption availability is something that they put a lot of value on.

sections.speaker: Catherine O'Brien
sections.content: And just 1 quick one on Netpoints if you don't mind. So really it's only been since April 15, that Netpoints sort of available to convert into Smiles points, but have you noticed, that I know you said the product was going well, have you noticed that significant number of Netpoints numbers have been taking advantage on their availability of Smiles points. And on how quickly do you think you can start involving those 5.2 million Netpoints customers into the Smiles program?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Let’s put it the Netpoints perspective, when you look at Netpoints from a strategic point of view, we definitely see that they have a very good platform to develop the retail part of the business. But Netpoints itself is still a startup. Last year they had consolidated gross billings less than BRL 7 million. So they still are prime companies, so the amount of deferred revenue that they have that can be converted into Smiles is still tiny. The main principal of buying Netpoints is that when you look at the retail side of the business, they were the one in the market that they were doing to best job. So that's why that we had invested on the minority stake. We had the option to acquire control in 5 years. And we have a bunch of entrepreneurs that are doing the nicest job in the market. But still in terms of the potential for the redemption, it is still tiny. While our view of having this conversion with that, it brings a lot of value from the Netpoints product and the selling proposition that will make their lives easier, to sell Netpoints to other commercial partners. But it still, this is not going to move the needle for us in terms of the gross billings in the short term.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: Most of my questions have already been discussed in the Portuguese conference call, but I have one last. In the first quarter of 2014, the breakage revenues was boosted by nearly BRL 5 million, because of the expiration of some points that were -- or miles that were given in promotions and those miles had a very short lifespan until expiration, I just wonder if those promotions continued in the first quarter and if we can expect further impacts on breakage revenues in the following quarters for the same reason?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: The main promotion, that impacted the result on the fourth quarter and in this quarter, they were the promotions that we launched in the mid-of October together with GOL for flights that occurred until December 2013. So this is the major effect had an impact in first quarter, because you have a lagged period especially for the retroactive credit after people fly, they usually take some time to accrue their miles and then the miles expire and this promotion of 30 days later. We have started to test this concept, with the promotion with other partners. We further lent in terms of time to expiration, which are longer than one month. It was 6 months period. So that potentially can have an additional impact, more in this third and fourth quarter, because we sense that having a different short span for the promotional miles was a way to promote the program and give the benefit for the client that is engaged in terms of accruing redeeming. So that would make the promotions more effective. Okay. So probably, and this is a strategic, that we're starting to pursue. So potentially, you can start to have this effect on a more recurring basis in the financial equation.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: Flavio,  correct me if I'm wrong, probably not in the second quarter? It may happen again.

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: Yes, in the second quarter -- probably in the second quarter, as of today, we don't have any promotions that will mature the miles in the second quarter.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: And another question, it seems that you have been really pursuing that strategy of preferring healthy margin, instead of a very fast growth in Billings. Do you agree with these that you will continue to favor margins instead of growth if you have to choose between those 2?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: As we always said, margin is the priority. Of course, that we believe that we can return to the growth for serving the margins, and which is good is that we have the ability and we have the management in place, in order to accelerate or reduce our speedy develop growth. And as I said before, in this quarter for example, the second quarter, we are growing again in a very good way and I think that we can present a very good margin or conserve or have a very good margin this quarter again. But you are right, the priorities of margin, if we need to make an option, of course, margin is a priority.

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: It's Flavio, even if you look into perspective, we can grow -- we had a decent margin, at least in the way that we track the pricing for Smiles and the competitors, it didn't affect our overall competitors in the marketplace. Our -- at least in our analysis, it showed that in over 55% of the flights we have a pricing, which is equal or better than the competitors. So what we're trying to be, we're trying to be very surgical in terms of where we have the opportunities to expand the margin. But you know, we're not giving up on the value proposition for the final clients because we want to deliver a lot of value for the clients in terms of what he can get with the miles that he accrued at Smiles.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Just one point more, which is good is that we are well aligned with their line of company, they allow us to deliver new products and to have established a price and every time we're together discussing the opportunities. So I think it's very important. The aligning with the airline companies, allow us to maintain or to develop our strategy in developed margins and products and growth.

sections.speaker: Rafael Frade
sections.content: Just a follow-up from the comments from the Portuguese conference call, if you could remember us, where the -- your expectations in terms of the -- when the anticipation some banks will end and you comment that maybe with one bank now your expectation is that, it will end only in the first quarter of 2015, that seems to be longer than previously expected by you? I would like to understand, if you have any view on why is this maybe because other programs are gaining some share for example, the programs from [indiscernible] and [indiscernible] really because the level of expanding by clients are reducing. So if you could give us some color about that?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Rafael, if you look at the numbers, we had a run rate of around BRL 70 million, which was consumed over the last quarter. If you give the same run rate like the end of the second quarter, we would have around BRL 30 million to BRL 35 million. This remainder is the part that you know probably can take some more time to get used. In terms of the consumption of different banks and different partners, they have different run rate and one of the partner is not using as fast we had anticipated. And in our view, at least the discussions that we have is not that it's migrating to [indiscernible] or is more part of the strategy of trying to retain more miles inside the program. For us it's natural that different partners, they can have different performance over period -- over different period.

sections.speaker: Ricardo Alves
sections.content: Just 2 questions as I couldn't dial in the Portuguese call. So sorry, it's been discussed already. Just first, very quick follow-up on the Netpoints question, you seem to have a very optimistic view on the acquisition, but I also understand it's a part as you mentioned, so just wondering, if could  we expect that a positive equity contribution from Netpoints to Smiles in maybe 2015 already, you think that's feasible?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Well, you mean in terms of the amount, whether Netpoints contribute profit since 2015?.

sections.speaker: Ricardo Alves
sections.content: Yes.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Probably not.  In terms the impact on the bottom line for 2014 and 2015, Netpoints should be at to the bottom line.

sections.speaker: Ricardo Alves
sections.content: Okay that's clear. And just last one on the operating expenses guys. We saw meaningful decline on the SG&A as a percentage of revenue, I think it was about 10% or so, right, I mean for the client versus previous quarters, is this a level you're comfortable well, this more or less 10% of revenue?

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: Especially when you compare the G&A and all the expenses, with the last quarter, keep in mind the fourth quarter 2013, we had a substantial amount of expenses related to the marketing and to the campaign that we run, starting on the end of September. So that was what led the amount of expense in the fourth quarter to be at such -- at such order of magnitude. The amount that we have for this quarter, they are probably a little bit lower than our expectations of what the normal run rate is going to be.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Just to add some things, some comments, I think the fixed cost will be stable, the good news is that now that thing is complete. And we do not have an additional expenses. And of course, we can manage the market expenses, the cost is from our own, if you increase the costs through being in investment like in market campaign, so which is good. I think that in the next quarter, we have a little bit more expense, not enough, significant big loss something like that.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Again, thanks a lot for your support and we are very proud to manage the company at this moment, and very happy to delivering the results to our shareholders, according to their expectations. And I think that we have conditions to remain and in the path and at the same time managing very well our margins and our capacity to grow. And in the next quarter, the quarters coming, we remain in the condition to innovate and to deliver new products and services to our customers in order to continue increasing the engagement. So thanks a lot. We remain optimistic with the company and with the market. Thank you, very much.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content:
Good morning, everyone. It's a pleasure to share with you Smiles' results for the second quarter in 2004 -- 2014. I would like to start with a few highlights for the periods on Slide 3. First is that Smiles presented a net profit of BRL 64 million that represents a net margin of 42% in the quarter, very similar to the previous year. It's good that we maintained flat our margins in a good level. Other great highlight is that we're excluding gross billings that grew 25% comparing to the same period quarter last year and almost 10% compared to the last quarter. It is a sign of a good relationship with our financial partners.
The growth in international partners' redemptions is another important factor. We have been preparing ourselves by seeking new alliances. It is fundamental to our share on high-income customers in the upscale segments in the banks -- especially in the banks. We grew almost 200% comparing to last year, and our redeemed miles are read -- represents almost 17% of our redemptions. In other words, we are enhancing our product attributes and diversifying our redemptions. Our focus on customer experiences should generate value. And we launched our newest miles shopping platform. Now we have full management of our e-commerce for products, and this shopping will allow our customers to redeem their miles for more than 500,000 options. Another great highlight was the issue of our debentures. Seeking the capital reduction, we have the proceed of BRL 600 million at 115 of the interbank rates in Brazil. We have maintained our commitment of maximizing value to our shareholders.
Going to Slide 4. I would like to highlight our miles growth by billings and accrual. As mentioned before, we are aligned with the banks, and all of them keep a very strong program with us with plenty of initiatives. All of them value us, and we are always aligned. The examples we had this quarter were Banco do Brasil and Itaú, which offered very positive incentives to Smiles. We are very proud to present this growth because the miles that we accrue at this moment represents the revenues in the future.
On Slide 4 -- 5, I'm sorry, we can see the growth of our redemptions. There was some seasonality and impact from the World Cup in Brazil. This season is [indiscernible], and redemptions came back strongly after the World Cup when compared to the first quarter this year. In terms of customers, we reached 10 million customers considering Smiles exclusively. We had a huge coalition operation with Netpoints, the biggest in Brazil, and we will discuss that ahead in the presentation.
On Slide 6, we continued pursuing new partners. In the second quarter, we implemented Aerolíneas Argentinas, and it has brought us great results. Argentina is the second most important international destination for Brazilians, and Aerolíneas is performing strongly as a redemption option. We will soon implement 2 new partnerships, the first one with TAP, the Portuguese airline and the biggest operator between Brazil and Europe. The second company is Alitalia, also important in Europe. Together with Air France, Alitalia, Iberia, KLM and TAP, these miles we offer have greater availability to customers seeking European destinations. These partnerships are very important for high-end customers, and it gives us great advantage with the banks. It reflects our own accrual numbers with banks. It is not a coincidence. We grew 25% with banks. We keep our commitment to continue expanding that venue, probably 2 or 3 this year, we are going to announce 2 or 3 new partners this year.
On Slide 7, another great highlight for the quarter was the agreement with Cielo. More than a simple alliance, this business will potentially bring Smiles to all Cielo's terminals. Cielo's technology and commercial strength will definitely bring us closer to retailers. The main focus is tourism and entertainment, restaurant in hotels, and we are very satisfied to have this alliance with the biggest acquirer in Brazil.
On Slide 8, an update on Netpoints. We remain focused on expanding our operations with Netpoints, first time [ph] that we made an excellent investment. Together, Smiles and Netpoints have 18 million customers, more than 18 million customers, certainly, the biggest loyal coalition in Brazil, where number of exchanges grew more than 400% over last year. It is important to mention that Marisa stores had already added up in the program successfully this June. Soon they will be partners with C&C, Casa & Construção, on the construction repair. And we had some different supermarkets, a good supermarket in the countryside of São Paulo. We have no doubts that these will be the biggest retail company in Brazil in the future.
On Slide 9, the next slide -- the new Smiles shopping platform launched this quarter. It gives our customers a lot more choices, and it is important for our strategy. You can redeem your miles for products on online stores of Smiles, Walmart and many other important partners. Just to have an idea, we have 500,000 products available. Redemptions grew 87% comparing to last year. So we remain aligned in terms of strategy, seeking growth and market gains, while maintain positive margins, with a great agreement for retail and [indiscernible] this integration with Netpoints.
I am sure that our path remains strong. I am very confident in the future of the company.
And now I am -- Flavio Vargas, my colleague, our CFO, who is going to explain and to talk a little bit about our financial results. Flavio, please.

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content:
Thank you, Leonel, and good morning, everyone, to participate in this conference call about our second quarter results.
So jumping to Slide 10 and continuing our presentation, we explained the composition of our gross margins. Our 100% miles redemptions kept margins 2 percentage points higher compared to last quarter, generating a gross profit 9.5% higher compared to the last quarter's and more than twice of what we presented last year, mainly to the legacy miles effect. Our Smiles & Money margins were almost 10 percentage points higher compared to last quarter. The lower volumes experienced allowed us to consume more classical fares and, therefore, including their share which lowers unit cost of redemptions and increases margins. Even though we separate Smiles & Money and 100% miles redemptions, we can analyze consolidate redemption results and direct margins by the spread between miles and money net revenues and direct costs. On the second quarter this year, we obtained a 45.1% margin, 3.7 percentage points higher than the previous quarter.
Now jumping to Slide 11. Breakage revenue was very similar to the last quarter, with the exception of the real expired miles revenue, which were not recurrent with the same intensity this quarter. Our breakage rates remained stable at around 17.9%.
On Slide 12, we see the net profit breakdown. Our operation expenses remained under control with an increase of BRL 3.1 million, mainly due to consulting services provided and marketing costs. It's worth mentioning that within the BRL 33.9 million reported as income taxes, there were BRL 3.9 million worth of expenses that will not have a negative effect on our cost position due to the incorporation -- operation of the Smiles, of G.A. Smiles, that happened last December. We closed the second quarter of 2014 with BRL 64.1 million of net profit, representing a margin of 42.1%, in line with the margins presented on the previous quarters.
On Slide 13, we comment on our stock performance compared to Ibovespa, adjusted for the EBITDA as a capital reduction that occurred on July. I take the opportunity to mention that the capital redemption was concluded with the distribution of BRL 8.17 per share, which is aligned with the commitment to maximize value to our shareholders.
So I thank you all for participating on the conference call. And now I open up the floor for the Q&A session. Thank you.

sections.speaker: Catherine O'Brien
sections.content: This is actually Catherine O'Brien filling in for Mike. Just had a quick question on the year-over-year increase you saw on airline redemption rate -- excuse me, other airline redemption rate that went from 6% to 16.7% this year, we thought that was really impressive. And we're just wondering how much of that was driven by the implementation of your redemption partnership with Aerolíneas Argentinas, and if there are any other such partnerships in the pipeline.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: This is Leonel Andrade. We have difference coming in the pipeline. We are going to implement Alitalia impact that we have announced months ago or weeks ago. And we expected to have 2 or 3 new alliances this year. Probably in the next weeks, we are going to announce at least one more. Specifically about Aerolíneas Argentinas, this is a very good partner, represented something like 20%, 23% last month. And -- but Argentina is very important for us as a destination, and we are very confident that this number, we continue increasing this number, but we have got -- we don't have our guidance, but I think that this is very important in order to bring upscale customers from the banks because the customers from the banks, the upscale segments, they like to go abroad. So this is very important for us, and this has started aligning with our strategy.

sections.speaker: Catherine O'Brien
sections.content: Okay, great. And then if I could just ask one more. How large of an impact do you think your commercial partnership with Cielo will have in the second half for 2014, 2015? Any numbers you could put around that or any color you can give would be helpful.

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: Catherine, it's very early to say how much the miles can come from different miles partnership. Probably this year, we will be working more in the pilot phase mode, so we'll be trying to test the waters and see what is the kind of products that we can sell, how we can pitch the sales, how we can make the sales force engaged. So there's still a lot of challenge that we have to face together to put that together. But we are very optimistic because we have definitely worked on the 2 very important fundamentals to make the partnership work, which is to use the sales force that provides high scalability in terms of the rollout of the partnership in the future. And we have built the right incentives in terms of how we are going to share the result of sales. And so we think the underlying conditions are in the right direction, but I think it's still too early to say how much they are going to represent. We don't expect that to represent a very big impact on the gross billings in the short term.

sections.speaker: Catherine O'Brien
sections.content: Okay, great. If I could just squeeze in one last quick one. Do you think that your new e-commerce platform that offers products for redemption for as little as 100 miles will boost redemptions? And how would you say that margins on redemptions or e-commerce platform compare to the margins you make on air travel redemptions? And obviously, I know it's in the very early stages, but just kind of first blush results will be really great.

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: Cathy, thank you for the question. Probably [ph] when the e-commerce platform, in fact, we have revamped that. We have launched that 1 year ago. But after 1 year in operations, we thought that we could -- we would have to enhance the platform and try and even to change the business model with the commercial partners. So in terms of the actual cost for redemptions, we are pretty much agnostic. We make pretty much the same margin on the products redemptions than on the air redemptions. But in fact, considering the amount of efficiency that we have gained over the last quarters in pricing the air ticket products, the air ticket products, they represent an even lower per mile -- or cost on a per mile basis. But our principle here is that we want to be pretty much indifferent whether the client choose an airline ticket or a product,  and the client will have the free will to choose whatever he wants for the redemption.

sections.speaker: Unknown Analyst
sections.content: Can you please give me an indication of the money revenue or Smiles & Miles, what the pickup has been of that after the World Cup, and what you guys expect for the rest of the year?

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: Vio, thank you very much for the question. Probably if we look at the results this quarter, we recognized this Smiles & Money was soft, especially when you compare with the first quarter 2014, which was very, very strong. The way that we have positioned the product, Smiles & Money, it's like a cyclical product because when you have a lot of redemptions and a lot of demand, the Smiles & Money is a very great value option for the client, and then you see more of the redemptions. What we have seen over the second quarter is that this was a soft quarter in terms of the demand. On top of that, we had a lot of promotional activities, probably more than 35% of the ticket we sold in the quarter were promotional tickets. So that reduces the value proposition and how the clients perceive the value of Smiles & Money, and that has led to a softer result. What we have -- what we are seeing over this July and we expect that to continue in this quarter is that the amount of sales on the Smiles & Money has resumed to what we considered to be a more normal level, probably not what we had demonstrated first quarter, but still very decent results and at a different level than what we had on the second quarter.

sections.speaker: Edward Tomordy
sections.content: Just a follow-up on the earlier question on the redemptions with your international partners. Could you give a rough sense of how they break down between Iberia, Air France, et cetera? Is it roughly split evenly? And then could you also elaborate on the discounts implied in those tickets? If you could share any information on that, that would be helpful.

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: We don't provide a breakdown of what are the -- of how much each of the airline, but a very decent assumption is that the amount of redemption is proportional to the size of the flights coming in and out from Brazil. So probably, if we have to rank, Delta, Air France, KLM and Aerolíneas, they are at a different level than Qatar and Iberia. What we have in pipeline, as Leonel mentioned during his presentation, TAP is going to be a very important carrier and partner because they have most of the flights from Brazil to Europe. They are operated by TAP. So we are very optimistic about the potential of having this new partner with us. In terms of the amount of spreads that we are able to comment on those redemptions, they are probably in line with what we have with GOL. We have recently -- in fact, as of today, we have raised the prices for the consumer for their redemptions on the international carriers because we realized that we have a pent-up demand, but still, we do not control all the supply. So we recognized that this was an opportunity. But in terms of the general margins that we can comment on this product, the value for the client is good, but we have a very competitive cost on the other side to allow us to provide this value for the consumers.

sections.speaker: Edward Tomordy
sections.content: That's very helpful. If you don't mind, just a quick follow-up on that topic. Can you give us a sense for what perhaps the average ticket price was for your international redemptions to what the actual market price would have been for the ticket?

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: Unfortunately not, but you probably can try to -- give you a number. We were selling a ticket from Brazil to the U.S. for 25,000 miles, and we could make a margin similar to what we had with GOL. So probably you can reverse-engineer and calculate what is the actual price of a ticket. Unfortunately, we have the confidentiality on the conference [ph] that we cannot disclose.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Thanks a lot. I'd like to give just some comments. I think that we have a very good second quarter, especially considering the margins. We remain as a -- we remain sustaining our margins. The accrual was very good, especially considering the gross billings from the banks. And the international partners, in terms of redemption, was a very good news. On the other hand, we know that we need to improve our performance in terms of Smiles & Money, which was very impacted by the World Cup. And the good news is that in July, we had recovered this performance. Now we are in line with the previous performance. July was in fact a very good month, considering the accrual, redemptions, Smiles & Money performing, which is good. And we need to take care, we need to pay attention in terms of promotions in order to sustain and increase our pricing to the banks and to the markets in terms of miles issued. So -- but I am very confident in our future. I know that we are going to have a very good performance in the next quarter. And I am very confident with my team. And thanks for the support from you, the shareholders and the analysts. So you can count on us anytime [ph]. Thank you very much, again.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content:
Good morning, everyone. Thank you very much for your presence here today, the presence on behalf of Smiles to being here with you to present our results of the third quarter of 2014. And I have the satisfaction of being here once again, emphasizing that our results of the third quarter are in line with our plans, focusing on our operation. Our operating income, if you look on Slide 3, was BRL 69.1 million, which projects an operating margin of 30.8%, 6% points higher than the same period last year. Our miles -- ex-GOL miles gross billings was BRL 219 million, a substantial growth of almost 27% that compares to the same period last year. And 24.7% as compared to the second quarter to the -- previous quarter this year. This is a reflect of the BRL 11.5 billion of accrued miles, our historical record, 12% higher than the previous year. And based on the growth in prices of the miles sold, therefore, our billings and our revenue are even higher than just the miles. This agrees with our strategy of working very closely with banks, our greater engagement with customers and diversification of products and programs performed by our commercial team. We have also had, at the same time, strong increase of customer engagement reflected in the miles redeemed, almost 10 billion miles redeemed in the period, almost 14% year-on-year comparing to the same period last year, a substantial growth of 33% as compared to the second quarter. 
In this manner, our cash generation, operating cash generation was BRL 170 million, a substantial growth of 34% as compared to the second quarter. And our net income was BRL 59.6 million is slightly lower, 5% lower than the same quarter last year. But, we emphasize that this reflects the new capital structure of the company after the capital reduction. We should emphasize once again, that we have done a capital reduction with a devolution of dividends to our customers. And in this manner, we now have, since the IPO, and the IPO in April 29 last year, so about 1.5 years ago our shares were sold by BRL 21.7 and now dividends are about BRL 10. And so there is a return for investments that is very strong. We have also launched in this period, a campaign, a marketing campaign called 365 Reasons to Smile. This campaign has provided substantial increases in access to our customers, bringing many new customers. This is a daily campaign involving many employees of the company because every single day, we need to have a promotion, a reason, something new, a new program and this is daily measured. It's a campaign that is not institutional. It's not a brand campaign, it's a product sale campaign for customer engagement and that has substantially provided reflexes in the results. We are going to continue the campaign. Obviously it's only 2 months old, and we celebrate every day until August 31 next year as we affirm the public commitment to our customers. 
Now on Slide #4. You can see the reflex of this growing revenue or growing billings. As you can see, on the chart on left, 26.8% as compared to the previous year and 24.7% as compared to the previous quarter. In fact, we have had an extremely strong performance in the sale of miles, bringing new customers to the company and driving prices up. The prices that we manage to transfer through our projects. Accrued miles, therefore, increased 19.9% almost 20%, again, emphasizing a strong growth engagement program with all our partners. All of them grew without distinction and the reflex of this is in our accrued miles. 
So high engagement of our customers on Slide 5 shows clearly, the redeemed miles. So our relationship between accumulation and redemption has been strong. And miles redemption has been growing 13% year-on-year. And obviously, we include seasonality in our engagement efforts and growing 33% as compared to the second quarter this year. And the number of members exceeded 10 million and we should emphasize that this is not our main growth measure, but the number of customers grew 5%, whereas miles redeemed grew 13% and miles accumulated grew almost 20%. Everything, excluding GOL, this shows that we have managed to substantially increase activation of customers per ticket and this is one of the main measures that we have implemented to improve the program. 
On Slide #6, I would like to emphasize, once again, the campaign, the 365 Reasons, this campaign, in addition of being a marketing program, there is a whole program of engagement with the customer. All employees are involved because every day, we need to upload a novelty for our customers and for the markets to -- and to announce this novelty so there is a process, it is almost an engineering process involving all the departments in our company. And there is a governance over these campaigns, so that for example, today, the last day of October, all the reasons up to December 31 are already being worked to be announced to customers. And we're going to go on like this until August, as I said.
On Slide #7, emphasizing that we have developed a strategic plan and we are sticking to it strictly and our strategic plan assumes strong engagement with end customers, improvement of quality, communications and projects. And we are on that track and being present everywhere by developing new programs. And this has been very successful from the standpoint of airlines. I'll focus on that more lately -- later. But in terms of retail, we have Netpoints. And on Smiles, we only focus on large retailers, the best were part of our target. So it's a great pleasure to emphasize that we announced a partnership with Fast Shop. Fast Shop is the top class or high-end retailer in Brazil for electronics. And with Fast Shop, we are going to develop many different actions for promotion. And we also have growth and alliance with Magazine Luiza, which has been a highlight, reason #12 and Magazine Luiza is another strategic partnership in our e-commerce. 
On Slide #8, our airline partners. We have been growing substantially in our international partners. To give you an idea, our redemption by international partners, excluding GOL, obviously. That's grown 98%, so twice as much as last year. And we are going to continue very focused, bringing in new partners. With new partners and with a substantial increase of availability, as part of international redemptions, it's clearer and clearer, the engagement of high-income customers. So in this quarter, we have contacted many different actions for high-income customers, such as Prime and [indiscernible] and other banks operating with high-income customers. And the type of agreement has gone up because of our penetration in high-income groups. We have 4 partnerships being implemented. All of them will be available for this -- for our customers this year. We are talking about TAP, the largest airline between Brazil and Europe, Alitalia, Etihad and Aeromexico and we have the intention of announcing new alliances and we are working on that. 
Lastly, on my part, on Slide #9, talking about Netpoints, our investment in retail. Netpoints went from 10 million customers in this quarter, they're doing very well. And in this manner today, we have the largest customer base. We have 19 customers adding up Netpoints and Smiles, 19 million customers and therefore, we are the largest combination or joint venture, so to speak, in terms of customers. The number of exchanges grew 817% year-on-year and we are closing new partnerships with supermarkets, a chain -- a large chain of gyms and Mr.Beer and many other ones and we are going to continue. And we are sure that Netpoints will soon become the largest retail mileage points in Brazil. And we are very happy to be operating together. 
Now I'm going to turn the conference over to Flavio Vargas, and he will be focusing more on financial aspects. And then we will be open for questions that you may have. Thank you, all, very much. Now Flavio, please?

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content:
Leonel, thank you very much. Good morning, everyone. It's a pleasure to be here today with you to share a little bit of our results of the third quarter 2014. So continuing the presentation, now on Slide #10, you can see the breakdown of our gross margin. Our redemptions 100% miles kept a margin of 38.1%, more than 10 percentage points greater than the same quarter in 2013, thereby providing a gross profit of BRL 51 million, 30% above the first quarter of 2014 and more than twice greater than the results in the second quarter of 2013, especially because of the effect of reduction of shares of miles in the redemptions. Our margins of Smiles & Money were 4.7 points greater as compared to the third quarter 2013. We should mention that the number of miles per ticket in this category has kept at a greater level this year as compared to 2013, thereby providing more options to our customers with comparable and attractive margins. 
Even though we separate Smiles & Money and 100% miles redemption, we may look at the results of Smiles as a whole with direct redemption margins this period with tax and direct costs. In the third quarter of 2013, our margin was 40%, 8 percentage points superior to the margins of the third quarter of 2013. 
Now going to Slide #11. You can see the evolution of our revenue and our breakage along the time. So the breakage revenue in this quarter was 33% superior to the previous quarter. And our breakage rate had a slight high of 0.4 percentage points as compared to the second quarter of 2014. 
Now going to Slide #12. You can see the net income breakdown. Our operating expenses are under control with the main variation caused by the increase of BRL 3.5 million regarding advertising and marketing expenses, with the launch of our new campaign. We should also emphasize that on this quarter, out of the 31.2% tax on profit, there is a deferred expense of BRL 3.2 million that will not have a cash negative effect because it is our operation of incorporation of Smiles that was performed in December last year. We closed the period with BRL 59.6 million net income and the net income of 26.6%. This was driven by the growth in operating profit and already reflecting the impact of the new capital structure after our capital reduction. We should emphasize that our gross -- net profit would be approximately BRL 63 million for the third quarter of 2014, which would represent an approximate growth of 20% as compared to the third quarter of 2013. If we did not consider the effect of the capital reduction, which had an impact of BRL 400 million in the company's own cash and the debt service regarding the BRL 6 million of the debentures that we raised. 
Now going to Slide #13. We present our performance of our shares as compared to IBOVESPA and adjusted. And so I take the opportunity to mention that since our IPO, we have paid out BRL 9.78 to shareholders, representing approximately 45% of the price of the IPO. 
So with this slide, I close our presentation. And I say that I am available to answer any questions you may have. I thank you, all, for your attention. Thank you very much.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: My question regards the unit costs, there has been an increase in unit cost for redemption and air tickets in this quarter from 5% to 6%, according to our calculations. In the release, you say something that this is because of the net interest in redemption mix with a smaller share of promotional tickets. What are you expecting for upcoming quarters? Should we expect this mix to be relatively worse or should we expect it to go back to the levels in the first quarter this year?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Thank you very much for your question. I think that after -- 7 conference calls to announce results, it's clear that our results and seasonality follows very closely the movement that takes place in the air market in Brazil. So if we are to remember in this last quarter, a weaker quarter in terms of redemption. In contrast, we had a lower unit price. So naturally, as in stronger quarters, such as the third quarter for the air segment, is a segment of recovery in the fourth quarter, is also a strong quarter in the air industry would be quarters when we have a higher concentration of a mix where promotional tickets are not slow offered, because there's not so much need by the airline. So if you look, not just at this quarter, but other quarters, we hope that there is a natural fluctuation in which the number of promotional tickets that have an impact in our redemption mix and our cost. It evolves along time, but this and all these movements reinforce our products. So they seem more attractive in high demand times.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: If you allow me to ask a second question. Do you already know if there will be any type of change in the policy to book breakage revenues that Smiles currently adopts?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Well, so far, we have nothing concrete to announce to you all, to everyone. We have been doing our analysis in terms of how we calculate our estimates to book breakage results. This is a discussion that we have internally. But there is still a process of discussion with our financial committees, with the board and auditors. So we cannot yet provide you any definition whether there will be any formal changes in the way we book our breakage revenues. But yes, there have been internal discussions about that.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: Do you have a deadline to make a decision?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: No, we don't. Well, it's going to be whenever we manage to convince our stakeholders about the best methodology. And so as soon as we have that definition, we will inform. We've been working, but it's hard to tell you when. We don't have a formal deadline to tell you -- well, by December 31, we will have decided that.

sections.speaker: Rafael Frade
sections.content: I have 2 questions. The first one regarding the units price of the miles. There has been slight increase in dollar in this period. And I was expecting kind of a higher increase because of the end of the advances that you had with banks. So considering quarter volumes, can you tell us -- there was a significant volume of bonuses? And the second question regards commercial expenses. So you had a significant increase quarter-on-quarter and is that just because of the promotional campaigns? Or is there anything else affecting this performance? And what would be a reasonable level for us to expect?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: This is Leonel. Thank you for your question. First, the thing regarding banks, we did not have any increase in bonuses in this quarter as compared to previous quarters. It might be even lower than in previous quarters. With regards to prices, in fact, we -- well, those anticipation contracts from the IPO are maturing and we're negotiating with each bank individually. And so our contract with Banco do Brasil had matured and we made an adjustment. But you still have Bradesco yet to mature and promotions from each bank. So we consider that this adjustment is in line with our plans. And the fact that it grew so much, the volume of accumulation as compared to price, this is very positive. There has not been any strong increase in bonuses. As to your second question, and Flavio can help me to explain, but this commercial increase is due to the increase of Smiles and operating expenses, marketing, of course, because of Smiles & Money. When you sell through Smiles, there's an increase in expenses because the money part, there are some transactions that they have to take place. And there's also the marketing campaign, where there was a stronger seasonal investment even though our plan is to keep, in up coming quarters, a constant level of investment in marketing in order to avoid the peaks that we had in the past.

sections.speaker: Rafael Frade
sections.content: Okay. Just in terms of the commercial expenses, you said a part of the budget is related to Smiles & Money. Can you share with me more details? So there's a component of commercial expenses, which is variable depending on the results of Smiles & Money. Could you tell us more or less how much it could be?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Smiles & Money is about BRL 300. So most of the evolution of expenses comes from the launching of the campaign and the marketing expenses at higher levels. And the rest is what we have to pay to credit card administrators, which, to us, is positive.

sections.speaker: Eduardo Couto
sections.content: I have a few questions. First, about Smiles & Money. With a strong recovery in the third quarter as compared to the second quarter, do you still see Smiles & Money strong in the fourth quarter? Last year, the fourth quarter was very good for Smiles & Money. And so I'd like to hear from you whether this product is still doing well and you're expecting good results for the fourth quarter. And as to the product, well, the cash -- the share and cash, is it important for the profitability of the product or not?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Thank you very much. The message of Smiles & Money is very similar to what we had last year. So maybe we have a weaker environment. So Smiles & Money, too, was also weaker because of the value proposition of the project. In the third quarter, there was an overall recovery of redemptions through Smiles & Money. And in the fourth quarter, in principle, it's also a quarter that is a more solid that we sell -- start selling tickets for the end of the year, for vacation time, carnival. So it's a period when, in terms of redemptions and ticket prices, we have higher prices for the tickets that are sold. So naturally, that stimulates Smiles & Money. Not just Smiles & Money, but also the overall redemption environment. And in terms of profitability, at least internally, what we've been trying to look is the overall component. So it's not just looking at the cash component of Smiles & Money, but as the number of miles per redemption changed considering the offers of Smiles & Money. If you look at Smiles & Money, just at the evolution of the number of reals the that we've managed to have, it's a vision that is kind of distorted. So the ideal is to look at the product in a global way, considering revenues from redemptions, but it's contribution in terms of profitability. So it's clear.

sections.speaker: Eduardo Couto
sections.content: Flavio, another question regarding revenues. The 15% year-on-year and it was quite strong revenue in the third quarter. So do you think that next year, you will grow? You're going to increase billing by 2 digits, 10% or more? So this increase in ticket prices results in greater loyalty with the mileage programs?

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: Thank you very much for your question. It's going to be a difficult year. I emphasized last year that this year was going to be difficult. But anyhow, we've been managing to have an increasingly good performance. But actually -- this billing is more related to the Brazilian consumption environment considering credit cards, because our alignment with banks is very strong. We have many programs developed with banks for the first quarter and it will be like this next year. It's going to be more competitive, but I think that we may grow. There is no guidance in terms of us growing 2 digits. But the main issue is we are not going to give up our margins. Our margins are a priority. We are not going to reduce margins to grow. For the time being, we've been keeping our margins and growing more than the industry, and this is good and we're going to keep on this track. But this substantially depends very much on the macroeconomic consumption environment in Brazil. I think that further on in the future, once we look at the overall scenario environment and how markets are behaving and how banks are investing in credit cards. We may have a better overview or vision of the scenario. Anyhow, we'll still be committed to our margins next year. And this is the most important factor.

sections.speaker: Eduardo Couto
sections.content: One last question, now talking a little bit about cash generation. You paid BRL 1.2 trillion in terms of capital reduction and dividends. You're still generating lots of cash. Could you give us an idea of how much net cash -- when you're going to have net cash again because this has a strong impact on profit. And when you're going to pay this debt of BRL 600 million that you raised, more or less, in the middle of the year.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Eduardo, thank you very much and I think, just as a reminder, the BRL 600 million of debentures, they are 1-year debentures with monthly amortization. So the speed of payments of these debentures is very high. So up to June, July or June next year, we will no longer have this debt serviced and then we start having again strong acceleration of -- in terms of cash recomposition or cash recovery. Practically speaking, up to the end of the first quarter of 2015, our cash will be quite -- we'll be quite strict on cash. Because you have the debt service of BRL 600 million and we also have on the horizon the potential payment, the payout of dividends for the business year of 2014. So practically speaking, we will effectively have enough cash and a capacity of fast accumulation of cash starting in the second half of next year.
