sections.speaker: Eduardo Couto
sections.content: So do you think you'll be paying out dividends already in the first half for 2014? Because I was thinking more in the second half of the year. But you think we could have something for the first half?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Well, we might. But this is something we are going to decide in the shareholders meeting that will take place in August.

sections.speaker: Sara Delfim
sections.content: I have 2 questions. The first one regards the accumulation of miles because [indiscernible] quite difficult [ph]  on dynamics. It's been growing quite a lot with banks and accumulation with GOL has been going down for 3 quarters in a row. It's different from what we've been seeing in multiples. So could you give us more details? And whether it's going to continue in upcoming quarters? The second question regards prices. We've been growing in terms of accumulation with banks in spite of a significant increase in dollar prices. What's happening is that the multiple spreads that it had as compared to your price, it's been going down. Depending on the air performance this quarter, I think that their prices will be similar to yours. So we need to observe from now on is that slowdown in the relationship or in the prices from banks will swap [ph].

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Thank you very much. This is Leonel. We'll be talking about Smiles. We had a reduction in the number of GOL and this reduction is part of the program that GOL announced in October last year. GOL changed its mileage program. And it's a better program for engagement, because the customers that are engaged effectively have better rewards. And GOL stopped giving miles in promotional actions. I think this is very coherent and deep down, it increased specially for executives and in high-income brackets that travel -- that fly GOL frequently. About the miles, because of the change in the program, but starting in the last quarter, the change started to mature and maybe next year we will not see this drop in number of GOL miles. So we are hoping that we might have some growth, but not really substantial. As a reminder, the miles from GOL are very important for engagement and not so important for the financial result, because obviously, they are the cheapest sales that we have. As to banks, in fact, the growth that you observe has provided a very good return to us. And we're very much in line, we're still working with all of them. And all of them have been growing. And therefore, I believe that we will continue having a good performance with them. But here, once again, emphasizing we do not have an intention of reducing prices. We want to keep our margins, first and foremost. On the other hand, next year, as I emphasized in the previous question, it's more related to the macroeconomic environment than to our relationship. So with -- it's closed -- many of the programs are closed and decided for early next year and also, for the rest of this year. So I believe, we will continue having a preference and a very good relationship with all our partners.

sections.speaker: Unknown Analyst
sections.content: I was going to ask about legacy miles. What is Smiles' expectations in terms of this percentage? What do you expect for 2015 in terms of legacy miles.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Thank you for your question. So formally, we'll only break even or 0 as of 2016, because if you remember the program in the end of 2012, we changed the policy in the program and then started having an expiration time of 3, 4 and 5 years, depending on the category of the person. So it will just take a while for legacy miles to get to 0. But for the business years of 2015 and '16, it will become less and less relevant. So in terms of us understanding the percentage of legacy miles from GOL, as part of Smiles, Smiles will not be so important. But mathematically speaking, we still need to deal for another 2 years with this adjustment of still having some miles that aren't redeemed that were related to Smiles.

sections.speaker: Catherine O'Brien
sections.content: I was just hoping that you could give us an update on your e-commerce platform. I noticed that your nonairline redemptions increased to 3% of total redemptions this quarter, up about a point from last quarter. Can you attribute to the success of the e-commerce platform or is there something else driving that?

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: Well, you want to know more about what is going on with our e-commerce platform and what has driven nonair redemptions and what's our strategy regarding the redemption -- nonair redemption platform. So Leonel is going to answer this question.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Thank you very much, Catherine. Our priority focus is on air. And we are very much likely of growing in air, specially with GOL. But not just with GOL, but with our international partners. So our customers that send miles to Smiles and buy tickets, they want to fly as a priority and we still have ample possibility of growing in the segment, so much so that we made the acquisition Netpoints because we considered them being the retail is fundamental, so there is no distraction in terms of our priority focus, classes A and B, of customers who wants to fly. We also made the acquisition of Netpoints. We should emphasize that it is important to have this platform, and this platform is getting increasingly more robust with the breadth and possibility of nonair redemptions. This is a platform, as you said, is getting to 3% redemption. But we do not have any guidance. It's important for us to have this platform, because it increases customer engagement, but our focus will remain and we will increase our non-air options in retail. But right now we're not forgetting our focus, and there's no reason for us to think more in retail customers. They go naturally because it's an option or because there are good offers, therefore, I think that our share in the retail is not likely to grow substantially, even though it's going to grow in absolute numbers, because we have a healthy pace of growth.

sections.speaker: Victor Mizusaki
sections.content: I have a question about the payout of dividends and now looking at the financial statements of the third quarter, about BRL 200 million of accrued profits, the fourth quarter and this year and first quarter next year, about BRL 300 million of accumulated profit, which would enable you to pay something early next year. Once that is decided in the meeting in April -- shareholders meeting, could Smiles pick up a little or take a little bit more debt to pay out the dividends?

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: Well, honestly speaking, today, in a pipeline, there is no discussion in terms of our debt. So we have conducted a transaction with debentures so as to enable the payment of the capital reduction in July. But today, we are very much more related to use our cash generation in order to pay dividends. Today, there's no internal discussion going on in terms of having another debt in order to payout dividends. So we think that, along this year everything that is going on agrees with whatever we think is reasonable.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: Since there's not going to be a call in English, I was going to ask a question there, what about the rollout of your partnership with Cielo? And are you expecting -- is there an expectation you want to share with the market in terms of billing or in terms of what this revenue may provide you?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Thank you very much for your question. Well, the partnership with Cielo will start a pilot test in upcoming weeks. In November, we're going to start a pilot for this alliance. We're focusing on Cielo. We are very confident that this is going to be a very successful pilot. I'm not going to tell you where it's going to be. But it's going to be in subregion of a very large city, focusing on Class A, the facilities focusing on Class A with strong focus on tourism and entertainment, and we have some indication that our process is ready to go. However, as this is very new business to us, and it's going to be very different in terms of our performance and incentive, distribution, communication. I think that nothing similar has been done before. And therefore, we are going to test it. We are excited, I'm sure we have the best partners, the best network, and we are very much in line. We are going to test it. This test should -- will start for a few -- will go on for a few months and will go on during the first quarter. And then, jointly, with the representatives of Smiles and Cielo, together, we will assess. But I think in the first half of the next year we will have very great clarity in terms of the behavior of this initiative.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Thank you, all, very much for your participation. I would just like emphasize a few points. First of all, we are greatly satisfied of being a GOL company and to being very much in line with GOL. This has reflected every day for our customers. We are very much aligned and focused on the substantial growth of our operations. We are going to continue working to have superior margins, but we think we can keep on the path of a growth that is higher than the market average, specially considering the financial market and banks, we're having them as partners. Our relationship with banks is still very strong. Our programs with the banks are strong and now our customers reflect that continuously. This has provided a growth that is greater than the market average. Keeping our margins, and with them growth, so with predictable performance, without any major changes. I would like emphasize that engagement of our employees, and also our board of administration. So we are very confident of keeping on this track in the future. And we are going to work towards that. I would like to thank you, all once again. We are very happy with your participation. Thank you, all, very much. And see you next time.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content:
Good morning, everyone. Thank you very much for being here with us. It's a great satisfaction for me to be here to share ideas with you and to talk about Smiles' performance in the last quarter of 2014 and the consolidated numbers for last year. We were very pleased to see the results of the quarter when our operating profit reached BRL 88.5 million, and up of 36.3% as compared to the previous year and an operating profit of BRL 22 million, one of the best results ever since this company started. We've had the best quarter ever.
And I am now referring to Slide #3. Our ex miles gross billings was BRL 227 million and up 30% as compared to the fourth quarter of 2013, and accrued miles excluding our parent company GOL with an increase of 20% as compared to the same quarter last year, reaching 9.2 billion. In terms of miles redeemed, we grew 10.3%, up over the same quarter in the previous year. It was 8.5 billion and we had BRL 225 billion of operating cash flow generation. 105% higher than the same quarter last year, strong growth. As I said, our net income reached almost BRL 82 million with a growth of almost 23% as compared to the fourth quarter of 2013. 
And we should also highlight upon operate [ph] evolution of our program, we also launched the new Smiles portal. And this portal along with our campaign, 365 reasons [ph] has provided to the company many access, a huge growth of access of customers and transactions, and I think you will be able to see that even more strongly reflected in future quarters. With this portal according to customer research, we will probably have the best environment for businesses with loyalty companies in Brazil.
Now going to Slide #4. We will be talking about billing and accrued miles. I think that there's a very important growth in billings with 30% quarter-on-quarter growth. And considering the whole year, we grew 22% comparing 2014 to 2013, excluding GOL. And obviously, it's important to exclude GOL because it shows our evolution in a market where we compete with other companies in the market where we have to be better and more competitive than our competitors. And we have grown substantially more than our peers, which is very fulfilling. Of course, when we look at accrued miles, the growth excluding GOL was 19.3%, another quarter where we grow 2 digits in any year we grew in excess of 16.1%. 
I think it's worth highlighting that from now on, after the first quarter this year of 2013, we will have a normalized analysis as compared to GOL. As a reminder, in the last quarter of 2013, GOL changed its program and improved its miles to gold and diamond customers and also making relative premium, relative to the air ticket. In this reason, we had substantial differences between one year and the year before. But from this year on, we're going to have normalized analysis, which means that we have a very positive data. We started the year very strongly again and I think we will continue to lead the growth of the industry in 2015. 
Now on Slide #5. You see a strong customer engagement. Redeemed miles has grown year-on-year by 9.4% for the growth of the year. In the quarter, the growth was 10.3% as compared to the same quarter last year. We have more miles than redeemed because usually, customers accrue miles and only redeem them later on. We have prospects of having very favorable revenue growth because customers are very highly engaged to the company bringing more and more miles. In terms of number of clients, we have grown 5.9% and with almost 2% growth in the year, which shows a higher engagement of customers. And now our customers have been activating more and more our program as compared to previous years. So we already have a very high penetration in classes A and B, our target, and this is not a priority. The company's priority now is to activate existing customer, increasing more and more customer engagement, especially in our Air business.
On Slide #6. We highlight the new portal. Of course, this took on a great involvement in the project with intensive investments by the company, both financial resources and people resources, and the new portal on air now has been getting very positive feedback. The most important point about it is that all our air partners are integrated. We are bringing in more and more companies and they're all integrated. And when our customers choose a flight, they have all partners in a single spot. This portal has made it possible for us without the customer needing to leave the environment where they are to buy a product, to sign up in a Smiles club, to use all the advantages that the company offers and even promotions, purchase of miles and everything, and still preserving their air reservation. 
For example, if they are searching a flight then they have an advantage, they can make use of that advantage without changing environment on the Internet. Another significant aspect. The portal makes it possible for us to have a much broader range of offers of flights and/or whenever they choose one date, we also show flights offered on adjacent days when we've been having substantial increases of 2 digits if you compare it to previous periods in terms of navigation, new customers and usability of our website.
On the next Page #7. We have invested intensely in having new partners. And as a reminder, when we are talking about retail, we have a strategic plan and we only want to have one partner in each segment for Smiles and, this partner should also be a market leader and we are proud to inform that in the last quarter, we have signed a partnership with local leases, the largest Sky rental company in Brazil and they're fully integrated to our website and it really complements the travel experience with our target audience and it really leverages our possibilities. So just as we had said before that we had a partnership with Fast Shop, one of the leaders, especially with our target audience being market leaders in electronics sales. Now, we have localized it and we are implementing, we will have a partnership with core, the leading Brazilian carrier in one of the leading growth carriers or operators of hotel services at core. So we have few partners but all of them are very significant market leaders and very well integrated.
On Page 8. Here we highlight again that our strategy has been very much focused and our execution capacity has provided more and more opportunities to our company. On the last quarter, we implemented 2 major airlines, TAP. TAP is the leader in transportation of passengers between Brazil and Europe. It's the leading carrier outside Brazil in Europe. And today, it's 100% integrated to our company. With TAP and with other companies, we closed 2014 with 86% more miles redeemed from partners excluding GOL in the air partners as compared to 2013. So we practically doubled our volume, and this is fundamental for us to improve our penetration in high income bracket. In this manner, we are already the greatest carrier for all Europe and we sell the most tickets or seats to Europe and right in the beginning of this year, we are going to announce another 2 partners, so there will be 3 new alliances that will be operational very soon. [indiscernible] airlines, which is fundamental for the Caribbean and North America, very important strategic partner. We also implement the Aeromexico, the best compliments very well for the same destinations, Mexico and North America. And we have also announced Korean Airlines. It's not 3, it's 4, I apologize. We also implementing HI [ph]. 
And therefore, we will continue along 2015 to increase the number of our alliances and offers for international destinations and this is fundamental for us because in this manner, we're leveraging our penetration in high income brackets with our partners, similarly to very strong and segmented alliances with companies such as American Express. So we have also implemented the Alitalia redemptions. As you can see on the Slide #9, they are very important and strong partners. They fly to 32 destinations in Europe with 10 weekly flights from São Paulo and 7 from Rio 17 weekly flights connecting Brazil to Rome and from there, to many other destinations. Alitalia is also operational as part of our global alliance.
On Page #10, I would like to report on Netpoints. We are still very sure and very satisfied with the evolution of our businesses with Netpoints. Combining Netpoints and Smiles, we integrated basis operating joint fleet, so we become the largest joint venture of loyalty in Brazil. We have 21 million of different customers and the number of exchanges with Netpoints has been growing significantly, bringing more and more partner in a significant manner. This quarter, for example, Netpoints brought a partnership with [indiscernible] and we should highlight that it launched the first road mileage program in Brazil, which is called Sharp Street [ph]. It's already operational, bringing activation and engagement of more customers in a very strong manner. So Netpoints is taking off and I have -- as I have always said, ever since the acquisition, we are very confident that Netpoints is going to be the greatest retail loyalty program in Brazil. And recently, Netpoints has announced a new partnership with a huge supermarket chain in the interior of the state of São Paulo and they have been announcing many more new partners. In the next quarters and months, Netpoints will continue to bring in many novelties.
All that said, I still believe strongly in our capacity to grow strongly in our industry. Our capacity of maintaining our margins 2015 will be a clearly challenging year. But our relationship, especially our unique relationship with our financial partners, we are very well aligned with all of them even with new programs and this has clearly translated in terms of accumulation numbers in January. And in the future, this will have reflected in our very strong execution capacity. Now, I'm going to turn the conference over to Flavio and he'll be talking about financial numbers and then we are going to have a Q&A session. Flavio, please.

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content:
Thank you very much, Leonel, and good morning, to everyone. Now continuing our presentation, you can see on Slide #11 the share and performance of our redemptions isolated from other effects. The 100% mile projects have kept a margin of 42% more than 100 basis points greater than the same quarter last year with a growth result of almost BRL 46 million, approximately 30% greater than the fourth quarter of 2013, especially driven by the effect of the reduction of the participation of legacy miles. The [indiscernible] Smiles & Money margins were almost 600 basis points greater as compared to the fourth quarter of 2013, with a growth of revenue in this modality around 50% year-on-year.
We should also mention the number of miles per ticket in this category has remained at a greater level this year as compared to 2013, thereby guaranteeing more options to our customers with favorable margins for the company. Even if we separate the redemptions from Smiles & Money and 100% miles, we should analyze the results of redemptions of miles as a whole. So the spread between the revenue from miles and Smiles & Money with taxes and direct costs. In the fourth quarter of 2014, we have had a 47% margin, almost 400 basis points greater than the margin of 2013.
Now going to the next slide. You can see the evolution of the redemption margin per quarter. When we complete 2 years as an independent company, we can show you better the gains in gross margin, the reduction of volatility in margins, which we managed to attain through an efficient pricing method.
Now, going to Slide #13. You can see the revenue from breakage, which was about 45% lower than previous quarter, especially related to a mild reduction in rate. Our breakage rate has had a drop of 0.8 percentage points as compared to the third quarter of 2014.
Now, going to Slide #14. You can see the evolution of the net income or the breakdown. Our operating expenses are once again under control with the main variation being driven by the increase of BRL 4 million especially related to expenses in advertising. We should highlight this quarter where we have had significant tax benefits amounting to BRL 10 million regarding to dividend payout on our own capital. We ended with BRL 81.9 million of net income and a margin of almost 34% driven by the growth of the operating profit and already reflecting the impact of the capital structure after our capital reduction.
Now going to Slide #15. We can see the performance of our stock as compared to I BOVESPA and already adjusted. I would like to take the opportunity to mention that we have at the [indiscernible] of capital reductions this year and we also propose for a decision in our shareholders meeting that will be held in April, the paying out of 95% of the profit that we have had this year, which is basically a payout of 100% if we comply with the minimum regulation for reserves. 
With this slide, I close our presentation and I would like to say that we are available to answer any questions you may have. Thank you very much.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: Good morning, Leonel and Flavio, congratulations, once again, on very strong results. And I have 2 questions if you allow me. I would like to hear your comments about the performance of unit prices in dollar for ex-GOL customers. And according to our calculation, there was a drop of 6% as compared to the previous quarter. How do you justify this drop? And what does the company express for a dollar unit price for 2015?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Thank you very much, Spada, this is Leonel. Very good question. Thank you very much for the feedback. Well, first, we should highlight one point that along the last quarter, there were some banks where the dollar was frozen as a result of advances of the IPO. One of those banks significant for us. They still have the frozen dollar because of the IPO. Now, this is over. From now on, we have no more that frozen exchange rate. But I would like to highlight the following. Prices in reals are going up and we might not be able to keep up with dollar prices because we have been working very intensely with banks with exclusivity when we've been focusing very much on marketing and Smiles promotions. And this has led our revenues to grow well above the rest of the industry. Of course, prices will go up and dollar prices will go up, but not 100% translation. I think this is an important part of our strategy. Obviously, so long as we preserve our margins and add value to our customers. And one important point, [indiscernible] is that, we have a revenue management that is very strong, consistent and a very strong alliance with the airlines, so that our work with inventory in our partners even though we do not transfer 100% of dollar exchange rates in the accrued rates, we have been able to manage very well advantages and redemptions especially with our products. So I know that, that we didn't transfer 100% of the dollar exchange rate in the last quarter and this was related to our rates with a significant bank and we will go on going up in reals, maybe not if 100% of the dollars because we're working on other fronts.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: The second question you mentioned in margin in your first answer. What are your expectations relation to gross margins considering the price increase of tendered prices for tickets as of January? Do you still want to have the strategy of not sacrificing margin if you need to grow less? What is your mindset regarding that?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content:
Very good question. Almost, I think, the same. We reaffirm, as always, our margin is our priority as compared to the growth. The good thing that our execution capacity has made it possible for us to grow strongly. And I think we will be able to grow in future quarters but margin is the priority. I think, considering the actions that we have in our price management both in terms of redemptions and not just the prices that we pay for, the tickets, but what appears to the customer and products, the leverage of Smiles & Money products is fundamental and we will go on investing in that. And as a reminder, after we changed the way of the selling our product, today, the product is in the website with its 3 offers for each flight, we repriced, we've been learning and we've been working intensely and now our TRM [ph] and our management of issuance has been getting better and better. We're still confident that we will have superior margins, there may be a slight variation here and there, but I don't see any risks in the short term with regards to margins. So we're going to keep our focus priority on margins.
If you look at the results in the fourth quarter, it reflects a direction that we have indicated to the entire market that we were going to start a work of, in terms of pricing. So then when we got to January, we didn't have to change it all at once. Now considering, and if we take the results, we have the margin we delivered in the fourth quarter. And if you do the math with and the increase of 18%, just for standard tickets, not affecting our margins, commercial tickets and doesn't affect our promotional tickets margins, we get to a margin level that we think is a sustainable level of margin in the long term, even considering everything that we started doing that we are still doing along 2015, we would go back to a level very much in line with very solid margins at a very appropriate levels.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: So translating your gross margins levels, that should be levered in 2013 and 2014, 46%, do you think this is a still a feasible number in 2015?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Well, it might not be as high as that, but our challenge is to keep on having margins that are quite relevant for our audiences as a whole. Considering the scenario we have in 2015 with a more challenging economy, the increase in ticket prices, if we managed to reprice the successful year we've had this year. We might not, -- definitely our objective is to be very close to what we have been historically delivering.

sections.speaker: Rafael Frade
sections.content: So before the previous question, in terms of costs. In the fourth quarter, as you said, Flavio, we prepare the market for these increases in 2015. So could you share more with us about your vision on retention as compared to the competition and how you're seeing competitiveness considering your adjustments? And the second part of the question, looking more into the future and into 2016, for 2016, are we going to start seeing effectively the breakage effects? I would like to understand whether the share of points that do not expire in 3 years but only expire in 4 or 5 years, do you have a significant share of those points?

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: As to competitiveness, we are involved in a daily work of monitoring our competition prices. So historically, that our quarters days, weeks, when our competitiveness is greater and in some weeks, we are less competitive. One of the things that we've been seeing, the price history is that we have had a pricing dynamics that is much greater than the competition. So we have periods of high demand for tickets and ticket prices in terms of selling our tickets for money is greater, so we have higher prices too and the competition have also higher prices but they have a slightly more stable behavior in terms of prices to end customers. So at periods of high demand, we might be slightly less competitive but so far, from what we've been seeing and the levels we have today, we do not have any concern in terms of competitiveness that we have today, that will be very much related to our objectives for growth this year. So we do have the tools to remain competitive. We have a very special relationship with GOL in order to be able to work on competitiveness because actually, we see that Smile's competitiveness is very much related to the competitiveness of GOL. So we have been working together to always be able to offer to customers the best alternative in order to attract as much miles as we can in redemptions, and to bring all of that into our system.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Flavio answered your question very well and this is Leonel answering again. If you look at the international competitors, we are very highly competitive. And along last year, we increased the average prices of our mileage and redemption where our average ticket remain very competitive with our Brazilian competitors. With regards to GOL as Flavio highlighted. Sometimes, we are very competitive, sometimes not so much, because this changes a lot, but there's one key point. First, GOL last year was a very successful company load factor and in the growth of a customer base, becoming that the leader in the number of customers transported. So we're still very competitive because our customers can fly. And in our research and our everyday relationship with our customers, they do not question our prices. Obviously, the one week may be better than the other, but we take advantage of the opportunities. Customers have been pursuing our program with good eyes as advantageous because whenever they want to fly, they can fly. Your other question regarding, if I understood correctly, the growth of miles of customers, or miles that will expire in 4 or 5 years. Just as a reminder, the rule that we currently have that miles will expire after 3 years for all customers and for gold customers, 4 years and for diamond customers, they will expire in 5 years. So this policy started in January 15. So all legacy miles generated until December 31 were generated according to GOL's previous policy and that they would expire on the anniversary of the customers' enrollment in our program, respecting 3 years at least. So according to the new rule, it will come into effect as of January 2013 and then there is a smaller impact. Now in answering your question and with regards to the new current role, most of the miles that are generated, they are generated for Smiles customers. So miles expiring 4 or 5 years, we don't have those numbers right here with us, but I think Bruno can give it to you afterwards. But our miles expire in the original, the time of the program after 3 years. There's another point that you should be reminded of that the miles that we offer to our gold and diamond customers, they are very much engaged customers. So usually, they will not leave miles expire because there's a smaller breakage freight. So here, miles don't usually expire for these customers.

sections.speaker: Unknown Analyst
sections.content: I have a very quick question. Could you tell us what your -- the share your international customers account for in terms of redemptions? I think it should be part of your strategy to offer to Smiles customers.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Thank you, Joel [ph], for your question. This is Leonel. So they're becoming increasingly more significant because of engagement of end customers. Remember that in 2013, when this company was separated, this number was about 5% redemptions. Today, it's around 15% of redeemed miles not in terms of the number of tickets because the ticket is greater. Something like 16%, and this is likely to keep on growing as we have more and more of these companies and we penetrate more intensely in high-income brackets. We don't have a guidance or a number to grow. I think that we will continue to grow but at the end of the year, this was about 15%.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: Smiles does not keep up with multiples in terms of diversification of redemptions, especially non-air redemptions. So as you said, you closed significant partnerships, including [indiscernible], Fast Shop, McDonalds, Netpoints. Is there any target in terms of diversification of non-air redemptions that you would like to reach by the end of 2015? What do you think about that? Especially in the short term, looking more into the future, what percentage of redeemed miles that you should have apart from air partners?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Thank you very much, Spada. Well, we don't have a guidance. We are very holistic today, considering our availability and our customers, they clearly want to fly. When we have good availability in the Air segment, we don't have great differentiation in margin in terms between air and non-air. The margins on the whole are similar between air and non-air. Non-air will continue to grow because we're bringing in new partners and this has been adding value. We do not have a guidance. This is not our priority in terms of growth. Much to the contrary, we've been saying all the time that it has been working with our customers and partners, especially a bank system. This is the place where customers can fly. And so much so that you see the evolution in numbers. The number was nonsignificant 2 years ago. I think this year it will be about 3, 3 something percent in terms of non-air but this is not something -- an area where we need to grow, at least not now. But they do add value, these non-air partners.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Thank you, all, very much and thank you for your support once again and for being with us. I believe that effectively, 2015 -- 2014 was a very significant and important year for the company. We've been growing and meeting the expectations of the market, especially launching new projects, increasing customer engagements. And most important of all, and we've always talked about this, is our operational focus. In Smiles, we work very much centered on not depending on breakage nor on exchange rate, and this is very much related to excellence of our products, how we've been working in terms of accrual and redemption and the offer of products, products that are more profitable and better for the customers. And in terms of availabilities of products for redemption especially, obviously, air seats. And this is all our customers want. I think we've improved a lot if you look at 2014 as compared to 2013, the greatest evolution is our focus on operations. And now we've been working on that in all areas and this is our priority and will remain our priority in 2015. I'm optimistic. Obviously, there will be some difficulties, but we can continue to generate value to our shareholders and high customer involvement over the next few months. And I would like to publicly thank all Smiles team for our support and our customers. Thank you, all, very much, and have a happy new year.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content:
Good morning, everyone. It's a great pleasure to be here with you. Thank you very much for attending our call to report the results of Smiles for the first quarter of 2015. 
And I'm going to start on Slide #3, talking about the financial and operational highlights. This quarter, it is a great satisfaction of us to announce record results of the company, excluding GOL. So we have grew our revenues in almost 61% as compared to Q1 last year, and we grew 20% as compared to the quarter previously before. This is a very robust performance, translating in 2.5 billion (sic) [12.5 billion], also a record of accrued miles. And excluding GOL, these miles have grown 45% and, therefore, very intense and consolidated work, especially with banks. And afterwards, we can talk more about that. And in this manner, we could have such an excellent performance to show more of our work and strategy. Operating profit is BRL 85 million, 24% greater than last year in Q1 '14, and our operating margin was 34.5%. Our first commitment has always been to maintain margins to work with higher margins, and we have been able to do that. And it was very gratifying to see our performance in the first quarter. 
Redeemed miles grew 13% as compared to last year. And here, we're also pleased to announce to increase a lot redeemed miles from our partner companies, which reinforces that our strategy is being successful, reaching 22% of miles redeemed from our partner companies x GOL. And also, our redeemed miles and our retail strategy, a very segmented strategy, just with large partners, reaching 6% of the total of redemptions have very great leap. Therefore, our redemption strategy is also successful. 
Generation of operating cash is BRL 335 million in the quarter, a very strong growth of 175% as compared to Q1 '14. Net income, BRL 70 million, 11% smaller than 1 year before, but this time with the new capital structure without any support or much less support in terms of financial results. The good news is that in 2 months, in July, we will settle the debentures and will no longer will have financial expenses that we're having now. And also, we would like to stress that in our offer of flight and seats with our international partners, we have announced Copa Airlines and Korean Air. Copa is already operational, and this is fundamental for our business.  
In this manner, now on Slide #4, we would like to emphasize the growth in revenues as compared to last year, 61%, very robust growth and accrued miles also growing 43.5% x GOL miles here, definitely showing a growth that is very strong as compared to the market. I would like to emphasize that this growth was very homogeneous in all sectors. We don't have one agreement with one specific bank. We grew very strongly with all 5 leading Brazilian banks, and we are very proactive in our agreements. And in the way, we have been trying to fast -- take over the liabilities because we know, as the exchange rate went up, the appropriation of liabilities would be fundamental for banks as fast as possible, considering, obviously, that all our customers will receive fewer miles per dollar spent. And also, the volume of credit cards is likely to be smaller from now on. So we were very proactive and very fast, and we had very positive responses from all banks. Further on, we can talk more about this in terms of engagement.  
On Slide #5, here, you can see what we said before. Redeemed miles went up by 13%. But there's a very important detail in all our businesses, both in accruing miles and in redeeming miles or in redemption transactions and accrual transactions, access to our website, our promotions and everything. We have always had at least a growth of 50% to 60% in number of transactions as compared to last year. So we have been attracting more and more customers to our business with a considerable amount of customer activation.  
On Slide #6, reinforcing our strategy of having more offers to our customers, our commitment is that customers will be able to fly with us and we have always been working on that. Our objective is to make them fly. We implemented Copa, which is -- with a very fast response, Copa Airlines add a lot of value, especially to customers who want to fly to the Caribbean or to North America with very consistent and very good offers. So Copa is very strategic for us, and it's already operational. Korean will also add a lot of value. We are implementing it. But it will definitely add a lot of value because it opens a very strong channel with Asia, including California, because there is a direct flight, São Paulo, Los Angeles, and we are going to have access to those seats.  
Another fundamental factor on Slide #8 is our partnership with Accor Hotels. It is Brazil's largest hotel chain, one of the largest in the world, and we have very aligned partnership with Accor, very aggressive with immediate response. And just as localized that we announced the previous quarter, Accor is fully aligned with our objective of making customers fly, and we are focusing on flier customers.  
On Slide #9, we are very happy to announce that the result of all the work we have done and especially the way we've been working with final consumers with engagement, offers, promotional -- promotions, communications and also focusing on quality of the services we provide, and we've been able to improve it greatly. Smiles has been chosen the 21st most valuable brand of Brazil, and most importantly, it is the brand that has grown the most. We advanced 14 positions as compared to last year. And 2 years ago, we weren't even in those ranking. In the year before, we were 35. Now we are 21 with a strong growth in the value of our brand. And now, it is the most valuable and the main brand in loyalty in Brazil. We were very happy to see this. And it shows once again that we are working very well with our customers, and we are getting recognition for this.  
We have just launched, as you can see, on Slide 10, new plans for Smiles Club. We did a lot of customer research with very positive responses showing that customers do want to have access to more opportunities of winning of accruing miles. So we have new plans. Now we have 1,000, 3,000, 5,000 plans. They are already available, and customers are migrating just as our new customers join in the plans with very consistent migration. And the great novelty here that we are launching today, it's on our website as of today, a very strong program of member get member for Smiles with Club miles for you and your friends, and this is what our logo or motto. And most of customers of Smiles Club spontaneously already recommend new customers. 60% were already recommending without any incentive. And now we want to reward our customers who -- that will win miles whenever they bring in new customers that -- and encourage them to join our club. And in this manner, we are offering new products, new plans and, at the same time, recognizing and thanking our customers for bringing new customers. It's the first time we have this type of offer, and I believe that this will provide great benefits to the company.  
I will be available to answer any questions. Flavio Vargas is traveling, representing Smiles in -- on an external event. And I have here with me, Marcos Pinheiro, our CFO and IRO, and Marcos will now talk about our financial numbers with more operational details. And then we'll have the Q&A.

sections.speaker: Marcos Pinheiro
sections.content:
Thank you, Leonel. Good morning, everyone. Now continuing the presentation, we can see on Slide #12, the share and performance of our redemptions isolated from other effects of the company's results. This quarter, the mileage redemption revenue grew as compared to the first quarter in 2014, whereas the money grew 7%. The direct margin from redemption is 43%, 1.6 percentage points greater than it was in the first quarter of 2014, thereby showing our efficiency in pricing and also both in miles and in money. 
On the next slide, you can see the evolution of redemption margin per quarter. You can see that we have reached the maximum historical level of 46% in the first quarter of 2015.  
Next slide, you can see our estimates for breakage for this quarter. Breakage revenues was superior than we had in the fourth quarter of 2014, especially due to the substantial growth of the gross balance (sic) [billings] of miles and also breakage rates in a period.  
On Slide 15, you can see the breakdown of the company's net income. Our operating expenses have had a decrease of BRL 3.4 million, especially related to the reduction of commercial and advertising expenses, in addition to our annualization of our personnel expenses. You should note that financial expenses are at the same level as last quarter as a consequence, especially, of the change in the company's capital structure. It's also important to emphasize the monthly amortization has less financial expenses to go down over the next few months, going back to its natural trend of the company's financial results. We closed the period with 69.6% million reals (sic) [BRL 69.6 million] of net income and a margin of 28.3%.  
Exactly 2 years ago, the Smiles started trading its shares in BM&FBOVESPA. And on Slide #16, you can see the performance of Smiles's stock as compared to the index in the 12 months that ended in the first quarter of 2015. 
Finally, I want to close our presentation. I thank you very much for your presence and attendance, and I am available, together with Leonel, for the Q&A session.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: Could you tell me what you think about this combination of very strong billings? Why do you think this is happening? It's something that seems to be not really logical for things to go on like this. Are you in any way concerned about this looking into the future? Because as you said, the price of redemptions, do -- is there a perception of smaller competitiveness of your products face other companies?

sections.speaker: Unknown Executive
sections.content: Thank you very much for your question. Well, first of all, as the accrual behavior was very unusual, on average, they take 11 months to redeem whatever they accrue. So normally, 9, 10, 11 months on average. So therefore, with this very strong accumulation, it's natural for the burden to drop. And another point is that we have been improving substantially our redemptions through diversification, increasing redemptions by 13% is not bad. With this accrual, we will probably have a strong increase of redemptions in future periods, and we can do that in spite of the crisis. Average miles, not the lowest miles, the competitors what we have today, and they're still investigating and looking and studying our competitiveness. This is very cyclic. Some weeks, we are better. Others, not so good. But we have the freedom to do something about it. I'm not worried about that. It would be worrisome if the next period, in the future, our breakage grows a lot and -- meaning that we are accruing, but not engaging customers appropriately. I think it's still too early, and I see accrual as something very good. And this will certainly provide us good additional results in the future.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: If you allow me to follow up, since you mentioned pricing and initial considerations, you mentioned Copa, the volume of redeemed miles from partner companies set a record in the first quarter, 21% or 22%. With Copa joining and also the Korean company, do you expect that this number will grow even further, maybe getting closer to 25% or 30%? Or do you believe that 20% is a level that we should assume from now on?

sections.speaker: Unknown Executive
sections.content: Thank you for your question. Well, in addition to Copa and Korean, we are also implementing Aeroméxico. We are in the final negotiation stages with Air Canada, and there will be changes with the other companies on the pipeline. And this will definitely grow. You can be sure about that. The issue here, it seems to be, that it might be still too early, but I do believe that we will have a reduction of demand of international flights because of dollars -- because of exchange rates in Brazil. And people will be preferring more local flights. So this will not affect the share of international companies. But then, on the other hand, what is going on, we are becoming a very strong program with the banks that operate with high income, American Express, Citibank, so high-income segments in each bank. And this has provided an average volume of accrual that is different. So high-income customers obviously can have much higher tickets, and preferably, they fly on international airlines. If the exchange rate wasn't so high and the issue of the macro economy, we would still be growing. I prefer to be conservative. And I think that this line will remain at those levels in future quarters instead of growing so much.

sections.speaker: Sara Delfim
sections.content: I have 2 questions. On Smiles & Money this quarter, you had a good share of money as compared to miles. So you have -- do you see this breakdown as a trend for future periods? Was it a special campaign, a one-off event? And the other question, how do you see the air industry? I think March was the first of many months when GOL had a small drop, and we've been seeing, indeed, that this number has been going down, both in the domestic and international markets. So do you feel any change in trends, in pricing of tickets considering the poor macroeconomic scenario and more consolidation so you might be able to increase the share of promotional tickets on the package as a whole and, thereby, reducing your average costs and then having an effect on your margins?

sections.speaker: Marcos Pinheiro
sections.content: Sara, this is Marcos. Thank you for your question. So first, a little bit about Smiles & Money. I think it's worth remembering the changes we made to make the product more flexible 6 or 8 months ago. So we created Smiles & Money that had lots of miles and slightly less money. Today, the offers we have for the customers who want to be engaged in miles and money, there are many different situations.

sections.speaker: Sara Delfim
sections.content: So you think that this trend will go on, will persist?

sections.speaker: Marcos Pinheiro
sections.content: Well, I don't see any strong reason for the trends to change. And today, they are around 40% with Smiles & Money amounts, and it changes a lot depending on the miles, lots of miles and some money. But today, our portfolio is quite flexible. I -- I'm not expecting any negative surprises for this product. Your comment about the performance on the air industry. One of our objectives is to add value to GOL. This is one of our most important partners. We are one of their preferential distribution channels, and we use all our business flexibility to try -- to fill planes in a profitable way. So if the time is for more difficulties in airlines and we are going to have more promotions even though the margin, as you said, is better over the average ticket, it is, at least in theory, smaller. But once again, I think that the tools we have here in terms of management make it possible for us to deliver stable margin at the levels you have been seeing.

sections.speaker: Sara Delfim
sections.content: And just one last question in terms of accrual that you grew, 44% with banks, a very strong number. Are you going to change for the year that? Could you give us a sort of any informal guidance? Are you going to grow by 2 digits, low 2 digits? Do you think Q1 was an outlier and then you're going to normalize and you're going to close the year close to what you were imagining before?

sections.speaker: Unknown Executive
sections.content: Sara. Thank you. Let me say -- make 2 fast comments about your previous questions. First thing, Smiles & Money, we will continue encouraging it. It's a priority. Customers love it. There is very high demand, and we are going to go on using our loyalty tools and engagement tools, encouraging this product. So today, it's more than 40% of our tickets, and we will continue with that focus. GOL, as we work very much in line, obviously, having the advantage of having cheaper seats is not a sustainable advantage. Therefore, our obligation is to try to help as much as we can. But then on the other hand, it reinforces our strategy that here, customers fly, and we will keep the commitment. If they want to fly, we'll do our best to -- for them to fly. As to accrual, I think we could make a few comments. The first one, late last year, it was very clear that there would be a high in the dollar, that the exchange rate would go up. So our commercial teams did very good proactive work. So at the end of last year, we went to the banks trying to realign and to have more options. Obviously, we had to give up, and we suggested it to give up this transferring dollars with a trigger and also a proposal that would need banks, in this case, to give us full preference. And they all did it without exception. As I've said before, there is no bank that stood out more or less. Of course, some banks made this policy very clear, for example, Bradesco. So Banco do Brazil also reduced the floor for Smiles, clearly incentivizing it. But each bank has a differential or one -- a different offer. What I think will happen in the future, and just a side comment here, with the higher dollar, it's very clear that customers will accrue less miles, and there was a liability that should be directed somewhere. And our focus now was very strong in the -- in bank liability, clearly trying to bring to us what customers already had accrued. So they will accrue fewer miles, but they are here with us already. So we had more than 70% transactions of accrual of miles as compared to last year. So there are many more costumers engaged. I don't think that we will keep at those volumes for a simple reason, first of all, because customers will win or will accrue fewer miles and also because of credit card volumes that will be smaller than we projected last year. But we are confident that we will grow above the industry, and we will still have a strong performance of accrual this year. But obviously, the curve will be different, the largest curve this quarter. We didn't grow so much as compared to last quarter last year. We grew a lot as compared to Q1 last year. So we have the same alignment. We still have the same preferences, the same incentives, the same dialogue, launching products. So I think that we have capacity to continue leading growth in the industry. I don't know if I answered your question, but...

sections.speaker: Sara Delfim
sections.content: No, you answered very well.

sections.speaker: Catherine O'Brien
sections.content: I was just wondering, what is driving the significant year-over-year and quarter-over-quarter increases in your other product redemptions? Do you attribute that to increased functionality on your web platform? Or do you think there's an element of a less confident Brazilian consumer seeing --  using points rather than cash as a more attractive option? And then just one more on this, how would you compare the margins on these other products to an air redemption, either at GOL or another airline?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: This is Leonel answering your question. Thank you for asking a question. I understand that you want to discuss a little bit how we've managed to grow in other products other than air products and, at the same time, whether there is any difference in profitability in different segments in terms of redemptions. As to your first question, I think that we had the merit of focusing on air travel. But we defined our strategy 2 years ago that the sectors that we call retail non-air, we work with very few partners but they would be strategic leading partners that are leaders in their own segment. So this has driven us to have a leaner team more loyal to us, so more engagement between us and our partners. The 6% growth was very strong, especially the average price of the miles redeemed in retail dropped substantially in this quarter because we have new partners that have joined very strongly, for example, Localiza, the leading car rental company in Brazil that is also related to our travel businesses, Fast Shop, Accor. So they all have very aggressive campaigns for us, also with a commitment of our loyalty towards them. If we look precisely 2 years ago, Q1 '13, we had more or less 96% of redemptions -- 95%, 96% of our redemptions were GOL, and now it's 62%. We had 3% redemptions international companies. Now it's 22%. And for the others, it was 0.5%, almost nonexistent, and now it's 6%. So this demonstrates clearly that our strategy, our capacity is very good. Now we -- and maybe the right word is like we are skeptical, we are agnostic. We all want to leave options to our customers so that all margins are more or less the same. The margins we have with GOL and peers and retail are very similar. For example, in our peers, the dollar rate may be -- may go up or down. So there may be a variation with internationals. With GOL, it may vary because of some promotion and also whenever some very strong strategic partner joins, offering very good promotions as we did this quarter with retail. But look at the company's strategy, we want our margins to be similar in all segments and to leave it up to the customer to decide. Of course, customers bring in miles, always with the intention of flying, and this is our priority. I hope I have answered your question appropriately. Thank you very much.

sections.speaker: Gabriel Gusan
sections.content: This is about the early or advanced sales of miles to Santander Bank. Could you give us more color about -- because this movement sounds quite unexpected.

sections.speaker: Unknown Executive
sections.content: Thank you, Gabriel, for your question. There is no intention in advancing miles no cash intention. This specific instance you mentioned took place as a result of a specific negotiations with banks. Last year, as I said before, we went to all banks. Some of them -- and we made different agreements with different banks, different proposals. And the intention of everyone with Santander Bank, at the end, the proposal was this for them to advance. They bought the miles in advance with cash, immediate, with the obligation from them to use all those miles this year. Well, they have to use them this year. If they are not used this year, they have already paid for them obviously. So strong growth as compared to the year before. This is the case of the bank. And this is not a strategy of going after more cash. It was just something specific. This bank made this proposal to us and we agreed with it, and that's what we did. As to what we're going to do with that money, we are going to pay our debentures by the middle of the year. We're not going to advance. We could -- they could have this call. They chose not to call for an early redemption. And then after paying the debentures, the cash will grow again. So this discussion is not in our agenda right now. I hope I have answered your question.

sections.speaker: Carlos Daltozo
sections.content: I have a question about customer activation. There was a substantial increase in the activation of customers in Q1. So what is the percentage of customers activated as compared to the total customer base?

sections.speaker: Unknown Executive
sections.content: Carlos, thank you very much for your question. I'm going to give you some information. Our main focus is not just to increase the number of customers because, normally, the 10.5 million customers we have, practically all of them overlap with our customers. So it's very common for people to be in 2, 3 different programs. What we really want to do is to activate those customers. This is the way we work. Depending on the criteria you use, we have 4 million customers that are very much engaged and involved. We grew the number of customers and transactions a lot compared to the year before. Our volume of customers accessing our website is 40% greater than it was a few months before because we launched it basically in -- at the beginning of the year. Our rates of redemption have been growing by about 40-something percent in terms of the most engaged customers, especially with this growth of the retail with smaller tickets. Well, this is the main information I can give you because the focus is more on activation. Have I answered your question? Please, if you want to make any additional comments.

sections.speaker: Carlos Daltozo
sections.content: You have answered my question.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content:
Thank you all very much. Once again, thank you, and especially thanks to our customers. I think that the fundamental fact for our business is that, today, we have control of our company in all senses. We have the capacity of managing our businesses. We have stronger capacity. We can keep margin. We are working intensively on customer engagement, maybe with more promotions to drive growth, very intense alignment, not just with the airline but also with banks. And this has always been a priority. And this -- so there's a showing the preference of the industry for us. And orientation is breakage. It's not a driver for the company. And sometimes, we might have a benefit not based on the exchange rate. But our strategy will never be based just on breakage and exchange rate. Our strategy is based on operational results and customer engagement. 
I would also like to thank, because today is a coincidence, we are celebrating the second anniversary of Smiles's IPO. It was April 29, 2013. And the return provided to our investors and shareholders has been very fulfilling for us in the company management, and this is a reflect of our inclusion in Bovespa Index as of next week. 
So thank you all very much, and you can count on us. And we will keep optimistic and strong for next quarter, and we have a lot of work to do. Thank you all very much.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content:
Thank you very much. Good morning, everyone. It is a great pleasure to be here with you today especially to emphasize our results for the second quarter of this year.
Overall, we had a very robust result with a strong performance. If we move to Page #3, we see the main highlights. Our total growth billings reached a record level of BRL 392 million, 66% up from last year. Our total miles accrued was also a record at BRL 13.1 billion miles accrued; a historical record, 31.5% up from last year. If we exclude GOL, we grew 38.5% in this market, which -- such a fierce competition, we grew even more. We had a very high level of redemption, 28% over last year at 9.5 billion miles redeemed. Our net revenue reached the level of BRL 275 million, 80% up from last year, also a historic record.
Our operating income, our main focus, our constant focus, something we have emphasized since the beginning, our growth, our sustainability, our rises is driven by our operating income and that income has consistently grown and has now reached historical levels of BRL 93 million, which means 93% up from last year. Net income therefore, reached BRL 89.4 billion, another historic record, 39.5% up from last year's first quarter -- or second quarter, rather.
All those points above also improved when compared to the first quarter of this year. So it's not only a seasonal event, it is a consistent quarterly evolution.
At the same time, within our philosophy of always being ahead in terms of innovation. We have launched a new product, a new experience for the product. We're talking about boarding fee with miles .We are the only company to offer that right now. And we also are working with a new redemption platform focused on travelers, working with Connexions, a multinational American company, which is very active around the world. I'll be talking about that later on. 
On Slide #4, I'd like to highlight our Reasons campaign, 365 Reasons campaign. In this first half, we had very good results. We believe that it helped sustain the company's results. Not only is it a marketing campaign, it is also a business platform and engages 100% of the company. Of course, we try to use a new campaign every day, 365, so that requires a lot of hard work from everyone, communications, marketing, service, client service, CRM, all the company's areas across the company. In this first half, we had 91 promotions, 32 promotions with final clients or end clients, 27 end clients with airlines. Those are campaigns geared towards the sales of products; another 27 with air companies, airlines, our priority; 16 promotions with banks; 15 with retail; and 1 about the Smiles benefits.
I'd like to emphasize that because we have no doubt whatsoever that this campaign was the sole responsible for the growth of the company, both in accrued miles and redemption numbers. 
In the following slide, we are now at 340 -- reason 340, which means that we're getting close to the end of the campaign. It will be over by August 31. We were very successful, I would say, in executing the campaign. Throughout those first 340 days, we had no mistakes. So all days including Christmas, New Year's, Carnival, we had new campaigns online and the company was 100% galvanized around the company, all 90 employees deeply involved in it. And the big piece of news here is that we have decided to expand that campaign. So instead of 365 reasons, we're now talking about 366 because we are talking about a Leap Year. Leap Year, so we start the campaign on September 1, with a couple of differences which will be presented timely. The concern, communications and other new factors, but the bottom line is that the campaign will continue. So we have Reasons to Smile now for 366 days.
And I'm sure this will remain as the main sustainability platform for our growth. Because of the campaign, when compared to previous period, we had more than 40% access to Smiles portal when compared to a year ago when we didn't have the campaign. Likewise, our number of transactions increased and we had 28% increase in miles redemption, which clearly shows a higher level of engagement.
On the next slide, boarding fee with miles, it's the big new thing in the period. We are the first company to do that, and we have been the first in other fronts as well. Very innovative company, but we're the first company to offer this option in Brazil. So our client has a cash-free experience. In other words, the client may be able to redeem his product without having to use his credit card; a very seamless process, very practical. That product was launched late June, and I'm sure we'll see results in the short run. And it's valid for 100% of gold domestic flights and also for reserves, so that's another product to make the client's life easier.
On the next slide, we highlight our relationship with Connexions Loyalty. Connexions has over 160,000 hotel options around the world. They are a global leader in the hotel market. Over 7,500 tour options, so not only hotels but also tourist attractions in a wholesale network, so chains are part of that platform and are now available to Smiles' clients. That's 100% aligned with our priority, which is the traveling client, and that has clearly resulted in growth in what we call the nonflying activity, even though our focus remains on the client who is willing to travel and fly. So that would complement our core activity.
Along with GOL, that's an initiative that came from the airline. We are strongly aligned with GOL. And so they launched a pioneer projects, they draw 100 -- raffled 100 million miles for clients. We had 7 weekly draws. 84 winners were announced already and that drove a high level of engagement that also helped GOL's performance to remain stable in terms of miles accrual, and we are very proud to be aligned with GOL. Our clients, especially the executives, show that they favor that airline. They have, of course, revamped their fleet. They have focused on their loyal clients. They have become more popular, bought new planes. And they have also been very active in the app industry, the app scenario. So this is another pioneering activity, which led to a high level of engagement and good results. 
And because of that initiative, we decided -- we're now on Slide #9, we decided to carry on with that. We now raffle 100 million miles every month. We now have a campaign, around the world with Smiles. In July, we raffled 1 million miles, so one of our clients announced -- it will be announced in the next coming days. And in August, we have communicated all our client base, all clients who transfer miles from the credit card to us will be competing for 1 million miles. So every month, we have a different reason, a different motivation, and we have a schedule for that throughout the second half of the year.
On Slide #10, I'd like to talk a little bit about Netpoints. Netpoints is our platform. We are also a partner, but it's our retail platform. At Smiles, one of the main reasons of our success is our focus on the traveler that has made us very competitive in the travel platform. And with that, we have dedicated employees for that, and Netpoints helps us sustain our retail business.
On this slide, we have a couple of figures, which are very positive. The first one, as we closed the quarter in June, Netpoints had 13 million members. When we group it together with Smiles, we reached 23 million consolidated members, which is the largest coalition in Brazil in the loyalty market. Without a doubt, we are the highest -- we had the highest number of members in Brazil. And most importantly, Netpoints reached a considerable number in their quarter, over 1 million exchanges in the period, which is equivalent to 16x the figures of last year. So it is an impressive growth and that has made us very, very satisfied, very happy. So again, I insist, 1 million exchanges in the first -- in this quarter in 3 months.
The bottom line is our focus on travel, has helped us achieve a superior performance in the industry with significant market share, but essentially based not only in growth but based on main thing, our margins. Our ability to manage operating margins is one of the company's highlights. 
And with that, I turn the floor over to Flavio . Flavio is here with me. Flavio will address the financial and operating slides, and I'll be available for questions at the end. Thank you. Flavio, you may carry on.

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content:
Leonel, thank you. Good morning, everyone. It is a great pleasure to be here today to share our strong results with you, the results for the second quarter 2015.
Moving on with the presentation. We can see on Slide 11 the evolution of our gross billings. We've reached BRL 392 million in this quarter, a growth of 66% when compared to the second quarter of 2014. Even though it is a seasonably weak quarter, we have presented a productive result when we compare the figures with this year's first quarter. The level of accrued miles ex GOL presented the same uptrend, having reached 11.2 billion miles, 38% up from the second quarter 2014 and also with a positive growth when compared to the first quarter of this year.
On the next slide, we can see that our clients have remained highly engaged in terms of miles redemption. The volume of miles redeemed reached 9.5 billion miles, a growth of 28% when compared to the second quarter of 2014, and also growing when compared to the first quarter of this year. Our net revenue, which presented a growth of 81% vis-à-vis the second quarter of 2014, was driven by the growth in redeemed miles by the evolution of the money program and also by the reduction of the participation of legacy miles.
Moving on with the presentation. We can see on the next slide the performance of our redemptions, excluding other effects of the gross results through direct margins that you can see on the slides. The premium redemption, which contemplates all redemption revenues, including money, presented a good margin of 41.7%, a healthy margin. 1.3% percentage points below the first quarter of 2014 -- '15, sorry. This result was driven by a good mix of products and also because of a price pressure from our international partners due to our currency devaluation, which has been kept under control because of the pricing done in miles. Our margins will be continued to be managed on a proactive way, aiming at a maintenance of attractive and healthy levels.
The breakage revenue can be seen on the next slide; 20% up from the previous quarter, mainly driven by an increase of rates in this quarter and due to a strong growth in accrual. Our breakage rate saw a variation of 0.5%, when compared to the first quarter of 2015, reaching 17%.
We strongly believe that the evolution of the program, the high engagement of our clients and the change in our accrual mix will continue to press our breakage rates which, in the long run, will remain at lower levels than those seen currently.
On the next slide, we can see the net income breakdown. It's important to see that our operating expenses remain, once again, under control and the main variation arises from an increase of BRL 5 million relative to advertising expenses. It's worth mentioning that financial expenses remain at the same level of last quarter as a consequence of a change in capital structure. It's also important to emphasize that the debt, which was higher in July 2014, was paid off -- was wholly paid off in July this year. Therefore, we can expect that the financial results will be gradually recomposed and reach levels which will be above those seen in the previous quarters, with a recomposition of the cash that we expect to see in the coming months, and also with different interest rate levels which are currently at higher levels than the historical ones. This way, we closed the period with BRL 89.4 million in net income, a margin of 32.5%, which was driven by the growth in operating profits and already reflects the impacts of our new capital structure.
Moving to the next, Slide 16. We have our stock performance compared to IBovespa, and adjusted accordingly. Starting in May 2015, we are now part of the theoretical portfolio of IBovespa, with an initial discount of 0.31%.
Well, with this, I close our presentation. And once again, thank you, and we are available for questions and answers. Thank you very much.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: Leonel, Flavio, my question is the following concerning the currency or foreign exchange. The negotiation with banks in reais is stable when compared to the previous quarter, even with a significant devaluation of the real. I understand this is a consequence of agreements made with banks, which are good for 2015. So my question is, is the current foreign exchange level, BRL 3.50, is expected to lead to any kind of change in reais going forward in the second half of 2015? I'll be back with a second question later.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Spada, this is Leonel. Thank you for the question. We do not expect that unless we see major alterations, but those numbers should remain close, the current number in reais. Actually, since the end of the year, when all the agreements were signed, all agreements are good for the year, no change in the horizon. When you see small variations, as we just saw, it is always related with the mix. Sometimes we have a product growing more than another, and this will lead to a marginal change in reais or reals. But I believe we should remain around that level for this year with a marginal change here or there because we have not frozen the prices in reais, but we have gradual steps to work around. So we have to have a very drastic variation. I hope I have answered.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: Yes. Yes, thank you, Leonel. I have another question, if I may. Within a few months, we'll see a new cut in the redemption of airline tickets for GOL, and that information will help define prices for tickets as of January. Do you have any indication, expectation on the product Smiles as to the magnitude of that price change for 2016 for standard tickets? Or do you think it's a bit early to talk about that?

sections.speaker: Flavio Vargas
sections.content: Spada, we are not, as of now, announcing an expectation as to that. But our feeling and our commitment remains the same. From what we've seen, things will be under control and will carry on regardless of the evolution of the tickets for GOL. So we don't see any impact on our competitiveness and neither on our operating margins.

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: I will just add a comment. Flavio is right. But just as a reminder, there's no change in contract with GOL. We are working under the same rules as always, and we are very much aligned with them. You know the realities of the market. So things are more predictable in that respect, more predictable than in the past 2 years.

sections.speaker: Alexandre Spada
sections.content: Okay. As to what Flavio said, he mentioned the maintenance of the operating margins. We know that next year, you will have some benefit on the margin because of the -- a maturity of a larger amount of miles. When you talk about operating margins, are you thinking about specific redemptions and not on the company's consolidated margin, which of course, will benefit from an additional revenue coming from the breakage? Am I right?

sections.speaker: Leonel  Andrade
sections.content: Yes. Yes. When we manage the redemption price for the clients, we do not include breakage as a variable. Of course, it happens every quarter, but it does not affect our view as to the redemption spread, right? So the breakage will not influence our pricing position. Just another comment, we are always focused on the operations side. We try to leave the breakage aside, if you will. So we try to be independent from breakage and from the foreign exchange rate. And we hope to remain the same.

sections.speaker: Sara Delfim
sections.content: My questions were already made and answered. So I'll look closely and if I have another question, I'll come back.

sections.speaker: Catherine O'Brien
